text: These Soundcore Q20i headphones work really well and do a great job blocking out noise. The active noise cancelling makes a noticeable difference, especially in busy or noisy environments. The sound quality is solid with good bass and clear audio, making them great for music, videos, or just everyday listening. They re also comfortable to wear for longer periods, which is a big plus. Battery life has been impressive, lasting a long time between charges, and the Bluetooth connection has been reliable. Overall, these are a great set of headphones that deliver both good sound and effective noise cancelling. Suggestions for improvement: Improve ANC performance for higher-pitched noises Add more padding options for an even better fit Include a carrying case for travel protection 100% recommend these headphones to anyone wanting amazing sound with great comfort involved. It gives you the option to use a cord, or go full bluetooth, not too bulky but still has a nostalgic feeling with the ear padding. The headset itself feels quite sturdy. Also, Soundcore has an app you can download to your phone that gives you some Equalizer options for more sound variables, and even comes with the option of having noise cancelling on or off, incase you want to still be aware of your surroundings if you are out on a walk with them on. It has the perfect use for those who... say, you use chrome tv or a tv firestick or something with your tv that uses bluetooth. Perhaps you want to watch something loud at night, but don't want to wake people up in the house due to the volume.. well, these give you that option. Kind of wish there were more color options, but traditional black is always a perfect choice. Honestly, if you are looking for headphones that will have an amazing life in 1 full charge, with perfect sound quality, versatile in its use, and won't break the bank even at full price, this is the headset for you.

text: These AirPods are my absolute favorite. I love these because unlike some Pro models, they don't come with foam or rubber earpieces which to me, are not very comfortable. Audio quality and features such as noise cancellation and adaptive audio are second to none. I also love the wireless charging case with speaker, so when I put it on a wireless charger, I get a chime telling me it's charging. Also in the latest released of all OS's, my devices tell me when the AirPods are fully charged. I'm very happy with these AirPods. My original AirPods were from pre-2020 I think? They were still working technically, but they weren t really holding a charge anymore and I had replaced the charging unit back in 2021, so it felt like instead of replacing that again maybe it was best to just get a new pair. I can t even express how IMPRESSED I was with the noise cancellation! It s honestly amazing coming from someone who has not had noise canceling headphones. The adaptive learning feature is also so cool - there s several features but I like the one that listen to hear if there s a conversation going and then it lowers the audio you re listening to. There s also a spacial awareness type one to hear what s going on around you, like you would without noise cancellation. But it s the noise cancellation that s the real star of the show. When I was on a plane and I put these in my ears without even listening to anything audio wise, it numbed the sound of the plane so much and made it so quiet it was amazing. They re small, and light in weight. The quality is just amazing - Apple does it again! They connected with my iPhone Pro 16 so well, instantly. text: I got these wireless TV headphones for myself, and they ve been a solid, flexible option overall. The biggest strength is versatility you can connect them directly via Bluetooth or use the included transmitter with optical or RCA cables (both included), which makes them compatible with pretty much any setup. I mainly used Bluetooth, but it s nice having multiple options. Sound quality is decent clear enough for TV and dialogue, but not anything exceptional. They get the job done, especially for casual watching. Battery life is excellent, and the wireless charging dock is convenient. They re lightweight and have decent padding, but after a few hours, I did start to notice some ear discomfort, so they re not ideal for extended wear. Overall, a practical and user-friendly pair of TV headphones with strong connectivity and battery life, just slightly limited by long-term comfort. I should begin by pointing out that these headphones are marketed as "TV Headphones", but that isn't all they're good for. Because not only do you have the "traditional" methods for connecting to your TV (RCA, 1/8" Phono, and Optical), but you also have full Bluetooth capability, which means you can also use them with your phone, tablet, or laptop. So, yes, they're excellent for the TV, but with Bluetooth on board, their usability expands greatly to cover pretty much any other device you might want to use them with. I'm a big fan of that, because if I'm going to spend $100 on a pair of headphones, I want to be able to use them anywhere, and these headphones I can use anywhere. The sound quality is really good, and the 3 EQ modes really do change the sound up quite a bit. I like dialogue mode for watching something where people are mostly talking (like watching the news), and either "standard" or "bass" when I want to watch a movie (which one kind of depends on the movie). The 20-hour battery life is excellent, though I really didn't push that to the full 20 hours because I simply don't watch TV for 20 hours a day. So, I can't say for certain if it'll go a full 20 hours on full volume (probably not), but as what I would call a "reasonable" volume level, I had no problem with battery life and simply popped them on the charger / base station every night before going to bed. Then they're ready every morning when I get

text: Good price and great audio. The headphones are also pretty comfortable. I was looking for something to listen to my audibles with that would cancel excess noise and be comfortable even with my tragus and conch piercings. Those piercings, especially the conch can be irritated with pressure on them for long periods but the ear parts are soft on these headphones and they cover my ear so no pressure. I use these in the house, mostly in bed but I've used them while doing laundry and they're lightweight. AI recommended these as a budget option for the Sony/Bose headphones. These are my first headphones since the 90s! They are well made and at $50 I think they're an amazing find. The headset is adjustable and the buttons in the external ear cap allows me to change from noise canceling to aware, play/stop and increase and lower the volume without needing my phone. Had these for just over a year now and they have held up great. They are a very high quality with a durable build. Plus I got them in white and they don t show any signs of wear/tear. I did buy a case to store them in, so that probably plays a role in that. They fold up to be pretty small without the case but the case adds some bulk if you choose to get one. These don t feel cheap at all. The sound quality is excellent for the price as is the noise canceling. I use these almost every day at work for hours and hours on end and they never hurt. The battery life is also IMPRESSIVE. I feel like I don t charge them very much and they last forever between charges. I sit in a cubicle where you can hear everything around you, but if I have music or a podcast playing you can t really hear any outside noise. Without music playing you can still hear people speak, but I ve had a lot of ANC headphones (and some much more expensive ones) that can t block out voices without music playing. I have a pair of AirPods Max and a pair of AirPods Pro and these JLabs are by far the ones I wear the most. I haven t timed anything, but I would say they are the most comfortable for long periods of time and have the best battery life out of the three. I never have any issues connecting or staying connected to my phone. Really I have no complaints. If you want a good pair of ANC headphones these are amazing

text: I've been down the 'Open Ear Design' rabbit hole for a while now, and there's two things you notice right away - since your ear canals are (hopefully) wide open... The sound is like a Bluetooth speaker invisibly floating in space that only you can hear. Pretty cool. Oftentimes, though, that magic Bluetooth speaker seems kinda gutless. Not so cool. Because of this limitation, I have tried to spend my way into something with genuine punch... And this one smokes them ALL. Regardless of price. I finally have the kind of bass response I can live with in a form factor I can reasonably forget about all day. They're on right now because taking them off makes no sense. The damn battery lasts 11 HOURS. PER BUD. No obstruction at all - I can use a Qtip with them on. It's fantastic. And the lows are REAL - usually open ear designs deliver low frequency sound the way sparkling water delivers the taste of fruit - barely enough to notice. Not these. Real fruit juice this time. Could they use a few extra features? Kinda yeah, especially for this price point. I also have a pair of Cleer's Arc 3 Sport open buds (every feature you can imagine but roughly 100 bucks more) - and I would really like to see the Shokz support LDAC, Hi-Res Audio, Dolby Atmos, and at minimum a more detailed EQ setup (5-band feels outdated, like a Sony Walkman from the 90's) - but the Shoks still sent those Arc 3's to the junk drawer by getting the basics absolutely PERFECT - fit, controls, sound quality are all in a class by themselves. If they ever roll out a Snapdragon Sound version of these with all the audio features and a case with a display, It's game over for the competition in the entire category. Multipoint works fine. It's a feature you didn't know you needed until you have it. I come home with these on (paired to phone), leave them on, then turn on the living room TV (also paired) and watch something too grownup for my little kids because I'm the only one hearing it. Dad Mode. Fit is impressive - they are comfortable, low profile, extraordinary secure (I can't imagine an activity outside of Jiu-jitsu or boxing that would dislodge them) and play nicely with both glasses and hats. Sound quality is

text: Here's my opinion and rating after using these non stop for about a year now Audio quality - 5/5 The audio quality on these headphones are phenomenal. Straight out of the box they sounded excellent, and provide a great neutral-feeling sound signature without boosting the lows or highs too much. Comfort - 5/5 The headphones fit very nicely and have rarely caused strain on my ears. The headband and foam around the ears are very soft. I have worn these for 5-6 hours at a time and have not noticed discomfort Build - 5/5 I've seen some people complaining about the build quality for the price, but I personally don't see their reasoning at all. The headset is made of lightweight plastic, but it doesn't take away from the premium feel and look. I love the fabric on top of the headband, and the version I have of these with the black-copper color scheme looks very fancy. Noise cancellation and transparency mode - 4/5 The ANC is nothing notable, but it is far from bad. I have brought it on planes and had no issue with the noise levels. Transparency mode also works great. Battery life and charging - 5/5 The battery life on this is insane. I can go about 2 weeks with constant use before I need to charge. They advertise something like 60 hours of battery life, and even while using ANC the headset delivers. Connectivity - 4.5/5 The reason I rated this a 4 is because I have had some issues with my headset connecting to my devices. Every so often my headset audio will cut out every couple of minutes for about a second. It's not a big issue, as it eventually remedies itself or I can just power my headset on and off to stop it, but it was worth noting. Other than that, connection is pretty smooth. I do like how my headset switches automatically between my phone and computer to whatever device is playing audio at the time. Extra features - 5/5 The app feels well-integrated with the headset, and it provides many options to customize your listening experience. I changed the touch control inputs on the side of the headset to feel more intuitive, as well as turning off some features I personally found annoying like the on-ear detection. The EQ also allows me to create my own sound signatures, and as a result I usually have the lows boosted. Other notable things - I haven't had many issues with the touch controls
product_name: Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless Noise Cancelling Headphones - Bluetooth Headset for Crystal-Clear Calls with Adaptive Noise Cancellation, Over-Ear Headphones, 60h Battery Life, Folding Design, Black

text: some features I personally found annoying like the on-ear detection. The EQ also allows me to create my own sound signatures, and as a result I usually have the lows boosted. Other notable things - I haven't had many issues with the touch controls, and although I probably would prefer buttons, this works just fine. The headset does have quite a large footprint when packing, so I wouldn't recommend them for travel unless you have space. Putting it around my neck feels a bit awkward compared to previous headsets I've owned, but maybe thats just me. These work great for gaming, low latency I'm just a regular user, not an audiophile. I mainly use these for listening to music and gaming Overall review - 5/5 (If you have the money for it) You'll have to sell your left kidney for these, but it's worth it for people who use headphones every day, all the time. There isn't a part of this headset that is not good, and it excels at being a comfortable pair of premium headphones. Before buying these, you gotta know some things. First, if you listen to earbuds a lot, over-ear headphones sound different. The sound is less directly in your ear, and more around you. As such, there is less impact. That doesn't mean these aren't good, they're just not shoved into your ear. Second, the source makes or breaks these. A crappy source will sound crappy on these and you'll end up blaming the headphones. It's actually a feature of the headphones though. They reproduce what you put into them very well. Go into Spotify and mess with the EQ there if you feel the need. I like sharp treble and got it that way. Change the stream setting to highest quality. Plug them into a good digital to analog converter (DAC). Try different sources and you'll see whatever you may not be liking is not the headphones. As an acoustic guitarist, I was hoping for the same sound I get with my Martin on my lap. I'd love to have headphones that sound like Tony Rice and Dave Grisman are standing on either side of me, but that's not gonna happen. Again, the source matters. These headphones are not a hand-built American acoustic guitar played by a master standing next to you, but they'll sound more like it than the majority of headphones. Third, we've become programmed to in-your-face sound. These Momentum 4 are very much "
product_name: Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless Noise Cancelling Headphones - Bluetooth Headset for Crystal-Clear Calls with Adaptive Noise Cancellation, Over-Ear Headphones, 60h Battery Life, Folding Design, Black

text: are not a hand-built American acoustic guitar played by a master standing next to you, but they'll sound more like it than the majority of headphones. Third, we've become programmed to in-your-face sound. These Momentum 4 are very much "grown-up" headphones. They articulate. They detail. The present. But they don't floor you with over-the-top sound all through the range. There are plenty of good, inexpensive headphones and earbuds that will pop your eardrums. I personally recommend Anker for that. My Space A40 earbuds are awesome, but when I A/B tested the A40 and these Momentum 4, I realized there were instruments I didn't even realize were there when listening with the A40 earbuds. Fourth, the bluetooth is dumb at first. From what I can tell, they won't work like you'd expect until you launch the app as part of the connection process. It's fine though. Just don't give up on them right off the bat because of that. Other random thoughts - they're super UNcomfortable right away, become very comfortable rather quickly - like within minutes. Also, good headphones do have a break-in period, and these are no exception. Be sure to try the wired connection on some different sources as well. MacBooks have good DACs in them. Anker makes a good USB-C to aux adapter with a DAC in it. The bluetooth connection can sound pretty dang good though. And the microphones in this headset are almost creepy-good. Also, bluetooth range is perfectly fine. I went three rooms away in my house with no issues at all. Summary - I was thinking of returning these, but I know Sennheiser so I messed with settings and connections and I'm glad I did. They're great headphones, but the key is to remember - if your source is crap, they will faithfully reproduce that crap.

text: Solid Gaming Earbuds With Some Trade-Offs I ve spent some time with the SteelSeries Arctis GameBuds, and they deliver on their promise of being a true gaming-first pair of earbuds. The big win here is the 2.4GHz dongle connection, which makes a huge difference compared to standard Bluetooth. Audio is lag-free, voices in Discord are clear, and games like Call of Duty or Helldivers 2 feel responsive without the usual wireless delay. QuickSwitch between 2.4GHz and Bluetooth is seamless, so swapping from console to mobile is easy. Sound Quality: For gaming, the directional audio is strong enough to pick out footsteps and environmental cues. For music, they re fine but not outstanding; details tend to blur together, especially compared to higher-end Bose or Sony buds. If you re a casual listener, they ll be more than good enough; audiophiles may want something else. Comfort & Fit: Lightweight and secure, but not the most comfortable for marathon sessions. After 2-3 hours, I find myself needing a break. They re bulkier than AirPods or Sony buds, so if you want a super-low profile, keep that in mind. Noise Cancelling & Transparency: ANC works, but it isn t anywhere near Bose or Apple levels. It s enough to dull background noise (like an AC unit), but don t expect silence. Transparency mode is functional but comes with a faint hiss, good enough for a quick conversation without removing the buds, but not natural-sounding. Battery & Features: Battery life is excellent, easily lasting through long sessions with the charging case pushing up to 40 hours. Wireless Qi charging is a nice premium touch. The SteelSeries GG app offers plenty of EQ presets for different games, which is a fun extra. Mic quality is decent at a desk, better than built-in laptop mics, but not on the level of a dedicated microphone. Build Quality: The earbuds themselves feel solid, but the case is plasticky and not very premium. Bottom Line: If you re mainly buying for gaming performance, these are an excellent pick: zero lag, easy setup, versatile across platforms, and strong battery life. Just don t expect them to fully replace high-end music earbuds or noise-cancelling headphones. Great gaming earbuds with unique features, but not perfect for audiophiles or ANC fans. I've used a lot of ear

text: These SteelSeries Arctis GameBuds have been a fantastic addition to my gaming setup! The quick-switch 2.4GHz + Bluetooth 5.3 connectivity makes it super easy to jump between Xbox, PC, PS5, Switch, and mobile without any hassle, I just switch modes and continue playing. The pairing process was quick and seamless every time. The active noise cancelling is surprisingly effective for earbuds and really helps when I m trying to focus in noisy environments. The sound quality is clear and balanced with good depth for both games and music. I also appreciate the mobile app support because it lets me fine-tune EQ settings and customize controls to my liking. The battery life is excellent, I m getting close to the advertised 40 hours with the Qi wireless charging case, which is super convenient. The case itself feels sturdy and life on the go is much easier because I can top up the battery without cords if I need to. I also love that they re water resistant (IP55) perfect for workouts or rainy days, and they ve held up great so far. Comfort is also top-notch; they stay in well during longer gaming sessions and the fit feels secure without being tight. Overall, these are high-quality gaming earbuds that combine excellent connectivity, great sound, long battery life, and convenience. Highly recommend for anyone who games across multiple platforms or just wants a reliable all-around set of earbuds. I have had these for about 7 months, and in that time I have had two instances where the case will not charge. It's happening right now as I write this. The support website for Steele Series is almost impossible to navigate, it took me so long to open a ticket using their automated "AI" system because my issue wasnt listed and it refused to connect me to an Agent. Once it did I was able to open a ticket but the charging function started working again on its own, so I didn't follow up on it. That was a bad idea because here I am now with the same issue. A factory reset does not resolve the problem and their support is impossible to reach. The sound quality is amazing, and there is no lag. But I wouldn't recommend them based on poor build quality and terrible support. These earbuds are comfortable and have good sound overall. (I don't use the SteelSeries software so they could quite likely be even better.) They can connect via bluetooth or the included dongle and switching

text: on poor build quality and terrible support. These earbuds are comfortable and have good sound overall. (I don't use the SteelSeries software so they could quite likely be even better.) They can connect via bluetooth or the included dongle and switching between the modes is easy. I am able to have them connected to my PC via the dongle and my phone via bluetooth at the same time. While I am not able to use both devices simultanesouly (could be a me problem) if I receive a phone call while gaming it does play ring sound in the earbuds and I am able to switch / answer quickly. So it does have at least some sort of simultaneous connection to both. I do notice that if I am connected to my phone via bluetooth and I walk too far from my pc it will play the "disconnect" sound when it loses connection to the dongle which can be a bit confusing. I did have one of the earbuds fail. The left bud had sound but was much quieter than the right using both connection menthods and various devices. I tried updating the software, firmware and completely factory resetting them to no avail. I reached out the SteeleSeries and they replaced them for me. The process was quick and easy. text: Solid Gaming Earbuds With Some Trade-Offs I ve spent some time with the SteelSeries Arctis GameBuds, and they deliver on their promise of being a true gaming-first pair of earbuds. The big win here is the 2.4GHz dongle connection, which makes a huge difference compared to standard Bluetooth. Audio is lag-free, voices in Discord are clear, and games like Call of Duty or Helldivers 2 feel responsive without the usual wireless delay. QuickSwitch between 2.4GHz and Bluetooth is seamless, so swapping from console to mobile is easy. Sound Quality: For gaming, the directional audio is strong enough to pick out footsteps and environmental cues. For music, they re fine but not outstanding; details tend to blur together, especially compared to higher-end Bose or Sony buds. If you re a casual listener, they ll be more than good enough; audiophiles may want something else. Comfort & Fit: Lightweight and secure, but not the most comfortable for marathon sessions. After 2-3 hours, I find myself needing a break. They re bulkier than AirPods or Sony buds, so if you want a super-low profile, keep that in mind. Noise Cancelling & Transparency: ANC works, but it isn t anywhere near Bose or Apple levels. It s enough to dull background noise (like an AC unit), but don t expect silence. Transparency mode is functional but comes with a faint hiss, good enough for a quick conversation without removing the buds, but not natural-sounding. Battery & Features: Battery life is excellent, easily lasting through long sessions with the charging case pushing up to 40 hours. Wireless Qi charging is a nice premium touch. The SteelSeries GG app offers plenty of EQ presets for different games, which is a fun extra. Mic quality is decent at a desk, better than built-in laptop mics, but not on the level of a dedicated microphone. Build Quality: The earbuds themselves feel solid, but the case is plasticky and not very premium. Bottom Line: If you re mainly buying for gaming performance, these are an excellent pick: zero lag, easy setup, versatile across platforms, and strong battery life. Just don t expect them to fully replace high-end music earbuds or noise-cancelling headphones. Great gaming earbuds with unique features, but not perfect for audiophiles or ANC fans. I've used a lot of ear

text: Solid Bluetooth Headset for Calls Great Battery & Comfort I ve been using the Poly Voyager 4310 UC for work calls, Zoom meetings, and everyday communication, and overall I m pretty happy with it. What I like: Designed for calls and meetings Voice and conference audio come through clearly, and the noise-canceling mic helps reduce background chatter. Long wireless range I can walk around the house while staying connected to my computer or phone up to ~164 ft away. Excellent battery life It easily lasts a full day of back-to-back calls, which is great for long workdays or travel. Comfortable & lightweight The single-ear design means I can stay aware of my surroundings and wear it for hours without discomfort. Multipoint pairing Connects with multiple devices at once, so I can switch between my laptop and phone seamlessly. What could be better: Mic quality varies Some users notice the mic needs to be positioned close to the mouth or adjusted for best clarity. Bluetooth limits audio fidelity This is really designed for calls rather than high-fidelity music, so if you want rich audio for media, it s not the best choice. Mixed review scores Some customer reviews report average experiences, especially with certain units, so quality can vary. Final thoughts: This headset is very comfortable, has excellent battery life, and is built for consistent call use perfect for work meetings, frequent telephony, or hybrid office setups. If you want a wireless headset that keeps you hands-free and connected all day, the Poly Voyager 4310 UC is worth a look. I have been a longtime Plantronics user and over the years dozens of people who have worked for me have used their headsets as well. I recently decided to try the Poly Voyager 4310 and I am extremely glad I did. I also purchased the additional power base for extended range and this setup is phenomenal. The sound quality is crisp and clear and nearly all background noise is completely eliminated. The noise cancellation is outstanding and the range absolutely blows my mind. I can move around freely without any drop in quality or connection issues which is a game changer for long workdays on the phone. This is exactly the level of quality I have come to expect from this brand. I have tried cheaper no name options in the past and they simply do not compare. Good things are seldom cheap and cheap things are seldom good. This headset is well worth the investment and I would highly recommend it to anyone who relies on clear professional call quality. I purchased this in May

text: in the past and they simply do not compare. Good things are seldom cheap and cheap things are seldom good. This headset is well worth the investment and I would highly recommend it to anyone who relies on clear professional call quality. I purchased this in May of 2023. It s now September 2024 and the headset has stopped connecting to my computer, wirelessly & with the wire. So it s the headset itself that no longer works. I purchased the protection plan that Amazon attached to this product, however, Amazon attached the wrong protection plan to the product so now Asurion & Amazon won t replace my headset even though I purchased the protection plan linked to the listing. Save your money and purchase from a reliable seller. Would NOT recommend purchasing from Amazon. 0/10 customer service on Amazon s part. Disappointed!! Not worth the price since it doesn t work longer than 1.5 year. Looks like there s other reviews sharing the same thing happened with their headset. Shop somewhere else!! UPDATE: Reached out to the manufacturer (Poly/hp) and they stated the warranty is for 2 years when Asurion said it was for 1 year. Asurion said I would need to use a protection plan since past the warranty window (which they couldn t even file a claim for) or to contact Amazon. After no help from Amazon, the manufacturer is replacing the headset due to it no longer working within the 2 year warranty time frame. Key takeaways: 1. make sure the protection plans Amazon links to products are for the correct item & price range, because they will not cover it when Amazon incorrectly links the wrong one to the product listing. 2. When in doubt, contact the manufacturer. (However, due to other reviews complaining of the same headset no longer connecting to a PC via wireless USB or wired USB after a year or so, maybe purchase a different headset to begin with.) 3. Amazon needs better customer service and needs to replace items when they are at fault for situations like this. The customer service from Poly was great.

text: Let's delve deeper into the Audio-Technica ATH-TWX7GY wireless earbuds, a true marvel in the world of audio technology. These earbuds not only deliver exceptional sound quality but also boast a range of features that cater to the most discerning audiophiles. First and foremost, the ATH-TWX7GY earbuds offer a design that seamlessly blends style with functionality. Their sleek and modern aesthetic is complemented by a comfortable fit that ensures they stay securely in your ears, even during extended listening sessions. But what truly sets the ATH-TWX7GY apart is their control options. Unlike many other wireless earbuds, which rely solely on touch controls, the ATH-TWX7GY earbuds feature both touch controls and physical buttons. This rare but welcome addition gives users the flexibility to choose their preferred method of control, whether it's tapping on the earbuds or pressing physical buttons. Even better, these controls are fully customizable, allowing you to tailor your listening experience to suit your preferences. Whether you want to adjust the volume, skip tracks, or activate your voice assistant, the ATH-TWX7GY earbuds put the power in your hands. Of course, none of this would matter if the sound quality didn't live up to expectations. Thankfully, Audio-Technica has once again delivered. The ATH-TWX7GY earbuds produce a rich, detailed audio experience that's sure to impress even the most discerning listeners. From crisp highs to deep, resonant lows, every note is rendered with stunning clarity and depth. Add to that the impressive battery life, with countless hours of playtime on a single charge, and you have a pair of earbuds that are truly in a league of their own. In summary, the Audio-Technica ATH-TWX7GY wireless earbuds offer an unparalleled combination of style, functionality, and exceptional sound quality. With their customizable controls, physical buttons AND touch, and comfortable fit, they're the perfect choice for audiophiles who demand nothing but the best from their audio gear. Out of the box, the sound is rather lackluster. Do yourself a favour and download "Wavelet" and adjust the sound via that app. The controls took a little getting used to, coming from different earbuds. Excellent sound range and quality, I can feel the bass, and they fit comfortably (after some time, more about that). Only two issues I had with them: First, ensure the contact pads on the earbuds are well cleaned (alcohol wipe, paper towel, etc) or else the buds can lose contact with the case and reconnect to the paired device. This is most notable when only using one earbud, as you'll hear the audio mute for a split second as if you were out of range from the earbuds. Second: For some reason, while using the soft eartips, my ears would ache like heck after wearing the earbuds for some time, like if you were to try and pry the inner ring of your earlobe apart with your fingers. Just that intense pressure feeling. Not entirely sure why, because they no longer have that feeling, though I'd chalk it up to my ears having a dissimilar shape to the earbuds, as I've had that same issue when I switched from normal earbuds to wired Apple earbuds a long long time ago.

text: Small and portable. Holds a charge well. Works great outdoors, originally purchased for use while in the pool. Also great for use while cooking or in the shower, with a clear sound and good volume, and is easy to carry around where needed. I have not tested the waterproofing, leaving it up on the deck while in the pool and on the sink while showering. For as small and inexpensive as it is it works great. Battery life is fine as well. Bass range is somewhat limited as it is in all small speakers but it's better than I expected. Also if you buy two of these speakers you can link them together for full stereo sound. I use this feature when I am watching movies in bed with my tablet and it sounds really good. The flashing LEDs are cute but if you are watching something they can be distracting, but you can turn them off so that's good. My one complaint I have is that there is no easy way to monitor its battery charge. You don't know the battery is low until it starts beeping at you. This speaker is great!! Would recommend to anybody. It is very clear, easy to hear. The Bluetooth connects automatically which is super nice. It automatically turns off after so much time which is super handy. Much better than any of my other speakers that I ve had and I super affordable! The battery life is great as well. Nice and light- easy to take with you when needed!

text: I saw mainly reviews from vine customers who got the free product. If you are like me and wondering if it s actually worth it, allow me to inform you, that it s actually very much worth it. I did not buy two units, nor am I an audiophile. I was looking for good quality speakers that had range and potency and that would cover a wide area. These speakers do not ever disappoint. They are loud, the bass sounds impressive and the mids and lows come through nicely. I used it at a picnic with friends and family and can tell you that with the bass boost on (recommended for outdoors) it was loud enough that I had to move it away from the group so that people could still carry a conversation. Battery does last, and the unit is hefty but it feels very well built. If you are looking for a comparison to the ranger from turtlebox, know that this unit has a woofer and twitter and dedicated bass speaker. The other unit has only a 2 woofer. The sound on these are far more precise and crisp than the other. Loudness wise, they are very comparable. There are many options in the market but if you look at these, get one with confidence, because you will be amazed. If you live in an apartment, be ready to get complaints if you have this unit at more than 40% volume. Wow just wow! This thing sounds great! Needed for beach as don t want to take our 2 Bose Revolve Sound links to the beach. I now have to get a second to pair them like we do our Bose. But serious folks this sounds better than our Bose Sound Links. Really does. I had purchased a couple of cheapies ones for beach some years ago that only lasted a year or so. Oh and these are hefty with weight. Made to take a beating and last ur appears. Don t hesitate and get off the fence and get one or two. Great product! Even though this is sold as an outdoor speaker, I actually use it indoors as a speaker for my turntable. The sound is unbelievable. I plan on buying another one and hook it up to this speaker. You can actually hook connect up to 100 of these speakers around your house. This feature is one of its selling points. You won t be disappointed.
product_name: TRAVOR Magnetic Bluetooth Speaker, 45W Rugged All-Aluminum Outdoor Portable Speaker, BassUp, IP68 Waterproof Wireless Speaker for Golf Cart, Camping, Beach, TWS, 25H Playtime, Meteorite Gray

text: I had high hopes for this Home 200 Speaker when I first unboxed it. Visually, it s a winner: it s compact, lightweight, and the sleek black shell looks great on a shelf or tabletop. Setting it up was a breeze, with syncing via Wi-Fi or Bluetooth taking just seconds. For background listening, the speaker performs quite well. It excels at capturing high and mid-tones, delivering clear vocals that make it perfect for podcasts, audiobooks, or genres like jazz and classical music. However, if you are looking for deep, thumping bass, this speaker falls a bit short. My main frustration lies in watching movies or videos. There is a noticeable audio lag where the sound doesn't sync with the visuals, and I haven't been able to find a fix for this. The instruction manual doesn't mention troubleshooting this specific issue, leading me to suspect the solution might be buried in the companion app. Speaking of which, while the app offers remote control and access to streaming services, I found it unnecessary to download another app just to control a speaker. Ideally, a physical remote should be included at this price point. Another design limitation is the button placement. Since all controls are on the unit itself or within the app, I couldn't tuck the speaker away on a low shelf as planned; I had to keep it in a visible, accessible spot. Additionally, it is not portable as it requires a constant power connection. While the build quality is good and it has modern features, the sound quality and lack of a physical remote don't quite justify the asking price, especially when compared to established brands like JBL that offer legendary sound at a lower cost. It s a decent speaker for casual listening, but I ve had to tuck it away for specific uses rather than making it my primary audio source.
product_name: Denon Home 200 Speaker, Wireless Speaker with Stereo and 3D Dolby Atmos Music, Home Audio Device, HEOS Multiroom Music Control, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, AirPlay 2, Charcoal

text: 5.3 : Pair the bookshelf speakers with your smartphone, tablet, Mac, or PC to play music wirelessly, no need to deal with messy wires.To use your turntable with our Bluetooth speakers: Your turntable must have Bluetooth transmitter/output(for connecting Bluetooth speakers and headphones). text: It is great if you buy it for the right purpose. I have purchased two sets. For casual listening to music from my iPhone or other Bluetooth devices, it is great. For my TV in my bedroom, it is great. Can turn up the volume and adjust frequencies with no distortion. It is perfectly fine for those uses. If you are an audiophile and demand perfection in frequency responses or in a home entertainment system with demanding requirements, you may be disappointed. But for the PC, casual use, Bluetooth, and normal TV uses, it is perfect. Great sound at a great price. Easy to set up. Easy to switch modes. And they look good too. Highly recommend. Good stereo speakers are a great investment. I've enjoyed my B&Ws for two decades. But having "a stereo" is becoming more uncommon. Many people these days only hear music through their cell phone or TV speakers. Perhaps a small BT speaker or a sound bar. I wanted to get my mom something better than her tablet to listen to music on, and was considering a single BT speaker, but discovered these sub-$100 bookshelf speakers. To be honest, I was skeptical, but the offer of physical stereo separation and bass ports was too much to pass up. No matter how cheap, when moving air there's just no replacement for displacement. They arrived well packaged & undamaged. Removing the grills for inspection did reveal a small bulge on the front of the right speaker, which appears to be a manufacturing defect. As it's not visible with the cover on, that's not a significant flaw. The finish does seem reasonably tough, a textured vinyl wood grain in a neutral dark brown that should go with just about any decor. Setup was easy, and it paired quickly with mom's iPad. I'm impressed with the presence of an optical input. It's not HDMI, but it wouldn't be hard to use the included optical cable to connect to a TV. Unlike a sound bar or portable BT, these speakers could serve as the center of an entire entertainment system: TV/gaming through the optical (plus music streaming with a smart TV), turntable or CD through the RCA inputs, and BT for phone or tablet streaming. No receiver or switches required. At this price that connectivity & utility is quite a value. So how do they sound? Pretty good. I'm sure all the components are as inexpensive as possible, but we've been making ported two-way speakers for decades.
product_name: Saiyin Bluetooth Bookshelf Speakers, 40W X 2 Powered TV Speakers with 4 Inch Woofer, Turntable Speakers with Optical/AUX Input/Subwoofer Line Out for PC and TVs

text: or switches required. At this price that connectivity & utility is quite a value. So how do they sound? Pretty good. I'm sure all the components are as inexpensive as possible, but we've been making ported two-way speakers for decades. You don't really need diamond tweeters to produce acceptable sound. People have been rocking out with the old silk domes for a long time. The bass ports are well-tuned & the low-end is as good as you can expect from a speaker this size. The mids are disappointing, but one thing I haven't seen other reviewers mention is the digital amp has three different modes: "movie/music/news". Clearly preset EQ profiles. I figured "news" would probably boost the mid/human voice range. Selecting that did help the issue, and I would recommend using that setting even for music for a flatter response. Some additional EQ tweaking would probably be a good idea, but I doubt my mom will notice. She would love listening to Dolly Parton on an old AM truck radio, so a slightly tinny sound is probably appropriate. Another option - if size is not an issue - would be to go with the 5" version of this speaker for a few dollars more. Nothing this size will ever compare to free-standing tower speakers, but every little bit helps. Just having speakers of any kind is a big step up from in-device (TV/tablet) speakers, and having two real two-way speakers is inherently acoustically better than any single-box solution, especially at this price. The appearance is good - to most people a speaker is a speaker - and the connectivity options are truly excellent.
product_name: Saiyin Bluetooth Bookshelf Speakers, 40W X 2 Powered TV Speakers with 4 Inch Woofer, Turntable Speakers with Optical/AUX Input/Subwoofer Line Out for PC and TVs

text: I purchased two of these a few months ago to use in my workshop - I cannot possibly rave about them enough. For comparison's sake, I also own a JBL Boombox 2, which I love. The D-10 keeps up with the Boombox on volume and connectivity without effort or noticable difference. True parallels. At times, I wish the D-10 had more EQ options, but that's a very, very minor complaint. You genuinely get 95% of the sound capability, for 20% of the price. Buy two and pair them together, you get 190% of the cound, for only 40% the price of a single JBL Boombox. It's an absolute no-brainer. Build quality is high, volume is obscene for something so small, everything feels very well built, and both of my D-10's have survived a couple of tumbles already. The ability to pair both of them together for stereo sound, genuinely the most underrated feature. It absolutely changes the game with portable speakers, especially not having to download a separate app to do it. 10/10. I have not tried the microphone function, and never will. I also find the LED lights to be unnecessary and a bit tacky - but they easily turn off with the touch of a button. (Still, I wish the speaker remembered that I turned them off, so I didn't have to hit the button every time I power up. Super minor complaint; but odd that it remembers it's last volume setting between uses; but not the LED on/off state.) Literally my only complaint, and it isn't even a real complaint. Of note, I have never experienced so much bass response from a portable Bluetooth speaker, aside from my JBL Boombox, and these D-10's. Especially in an enclosed room, it's pretty damn impressive. BUT WAIT, THERE'S MORE! After about a month of use, the end cap (which is basically a crash bumper) of one D-10 detached from the speaker and would not stay on again. Upon close examination of the snapping mechanism that attaches it, it seems that either the plastic injection molding machine failed to properly form a couple of the plastic clasps, or there may have been some damage in shipping. text: These speakers are quite amazing! I am a bit of a sound nerd, so I'm particular about spending my money on speakers. The solar power on these is what made me give them a look and then I read the reviews. Wow! Rarely do you find an item with 4.9 stars! All the comments were pretty much the same. Great sound quality and good looks, so I took a chance. I am certainly not disappointed! Listen, you're not going to get booming base out of speakers this size, but the low end it does have is very clean...even at full volume. I have been very pleased with the power consumption and the solar charging. I have used them for up to 8 straight hours and they were still at 30% charged. Recharging in the sun is quick! A have a suggestion... The instructions talk about hanging in an area where you can sit in the center for the best stereo effect. This is not always possible since deck seating can put you off center. There is an easy fix for this. Here's what you do: 1. Go to phone settings 2. Go to ACCESSIBILITY 3. Go to HEARING ENHANCEMENTS 4. Scroll down to CONNECTED AUDIO 5. Just slide the LEFT/RIGHT balance adjustment to make the sound correct for your seating position A couple of other notes. The lights are very nice. They light my porch very well. Maybe a bit too well. Hopefully future versions of this light might have adjustable light level. The LED temp is nice. I was lucky. I had some wall hooks from some solar garden lights that I have, but didn't use the hooks. They work perfectly to hang the lights! I also had an extra pair, so I hung those under my covered porch so I can get them out of heavy rain (suggested by manufacturer) and I can use them there when I hot tub. Overall, theses are amazing speakers... as long as your not expecting super high quality like an expensive indoor system. I will say that they are about the best I have been able to find, and they are solar! So far, so good. They seem to hold a good charge. Plenty loud from ~15 ft away. Pair quickly. Lights are bright.
product_name: pohopa Bluetooth Speakers Waterproof, Solar Powered 2 Packs True Wireless Stereo Sound 20W Dual Pairing Lantern Indoor Outdoor Speakers with 20 Piece LED Lights, Richer Bass, Black

That said, once I upgraded to a better amp, the difference was night and day. The speakers came alive and sounded amazing. text: I recently purchased the Sonos Roam 2 (White) and I am honestly impressed. Don t let the small size fool you this speaker delivers incredibly rich, clear, and powerful sound. The bass is deep without being overwhelming, and the vocals are crisp even at higher volumes. One of my favorite features is how portable and lightweight it is. I can easily move it from room to room or take it outside. The waterproof design gives me peace of mind when using it near the pool or outdoors. The Bluetooth connection is fast and stable, and the battery life lasts long enough for a full day of listening. I also love the sleek, modern design it looks elegant anywhere in my home. If you re looking for a high-quality portable speaker with premium sound, the Sonos Roam 2 is definitely a great investment. Highly recommended! The Sonos Roam 2 is a Compact Powerhouse for Audio Enthusiasts on the Go When it comes to portable speakers, the Sonos Roam 2 has carved out a niche for itself by blending sleek design with stellar performance. This second iteration of the Roam series builds upon its predecessor's success while refining features that make it an appealing choice for audiophiles and casual listeners alike. Let s dive into what makes the Sonos Roam 2 a standout device in the increasingly crowded world of portable audio. Design and Portability The Sonos Roam 2 retains the elegant, minimalist design that defines the Sonos brand. Weighing just under a pound, its compact, triangular shape fits comfortably in one hand, making it perfect for travel or outdoor adventures. The durable, IP67-rated casing ensures the Roam 2 is both water-resistant and dustproof, an essential feature for those who want to take their music to the beach, pool, or hiking trails. The slightly improved tactile controls and modern aesthetic are a testament to Sonos attention to user-friendly design. Sound Quality Sonos has a reputation for delivering high-quality audio, and the Roam 2 is no exception. For a speaker of its size, the sound is remarkably full and balanced. The crisp highs, clear mids, and punchy bass ensure an immersive listening experience, whether you re enjoying a podcast or a bass-heavy playlist. text: Okay where to start? We bought these for our wedding after the DJ raised the price by $300 dollars. After that we went to buy the Rockiville karaoke set but it soon sold out and we weren't able to get one. We finally decided on this set although it was more expensive. They shipped and delivered these speakers well in advance before the wedding so we could get some testing done even though the wedding was only in a few weeks. They were packaged neatly and there were not dents on the box. The wedding turned out great and the speaker quality was amazing. So now what do you do with the speakers after your party? Well we set them up in the living room and are using them for surround sound. So what do you need? A good quality optical cable, digital to analog audio converter, and RCA to XLR MALE patch cable. You can also get a USB extension if you want to plug your converter power supply witch is around 5W1Amp into the back of the speaker. Organize your cables with some hook and loop velcro straps and it will help keep your cables organized. These are also the same cables you will need to transform your speaker system to karaoke. All you have to do is look up the karaoke music on the device that hooks up to your tv. If you have a smart tv you probably have youtube. Theres plenty of channels that have free karaoke. Things you should know: -The XLR is for higher quality mics. If you are ordering a XLR cable. It needs to be a MALE. It's important because the first cable I order were females. -The microphone that comes with speaker system is junk. Order your self a new one. I went with the plye wireless microphone set that comes with two microphones. It was around $38. -Order a 1/4 to 1/4 audio cable to replace the one that comes with the speakers. Theres a slight buzz and I mean a very slight buzz but it gets worse as you turn it louder. A good quality cable will get rid of that buzz. These are perfect for karaoke or perfect for surround system. There also perfect for a wedding! My wedding turned out great and now I'm married to the love of my life! We are also lending the system to my brother whose getting married some time next year. Total cost priceless. Actual cost with all extra cables around $450 I love

text: Outstanding! For the low price (~$20), this is an amazing ear set. I have been using Apple Airpods and then SamSung Buds for the past 6 years. As expected, this set does not have all the features like Apple's or Samsung's, but having used this set for several months, now I am wondering if I ever really needed those features (for a price of $150-200!). Great sound, long battery, light but solid build quality, coupled with no fear of losing! The only thing is that when I drop it on the floor, it opens up. Each pod is made of two pieces, which separate as it hits the floor. After having to click them back together several times, I added superglue before click them back on both sides. Now they don't open up. Definitely recommend this set. I never write reviews, but I have to for this product. Just as most have said, these are AWESOME. I have had nothing but bad experiences with other wireless earbuds so I stayed away from them. But as my over the ear headphones of 8 years stopped working, I had to buy new headphones so I decided to give them another chance. I kept hearing good things about Soundcore so I trusted the reviews and they live up to the hype completely. The audio quality of course isn't the best, but it's clear enough, loud enough, and has enough fidelity not just for the pricepoint, but in my honest opinion exceeds it. But the most important thing of all, connectivity. That is the number one factor when looking for wireless earbuds. Many earbuds have decent quality, but if they don't stay connected, who cares. These always stay connected. Previous earbuds, the right would connect but not the left. This is because with other earbuds the right is the primary connected device, while the left is connected to the right bud. With these, it seems both buds are connected simultaneously. Also, these aren't just good for music and movies, even in games they don't lose their synchronicity. Only one problem, when I tried playing a PC game and watching a movie at the same time, the buds did cause significant lag and input delays in my game, but that is totally forgiveable. When the buds are dedicated just t your game and not to another app they work very decently. Most wireless earbuds I tried in the
product_name: Soundcore by Anker P20i True Wireless Earbuds, 10mm Drivers with Big Bass, Bluetooth 5.3, 30H Long Playtime, Water-Resistant, 2 Mics for AI Clear Calls, 22 Preset EQs, Customization via App

text: , the buds did cause significant lag and input delays in my game, but that is totally forgiveable. When the buds are dedicated just t your game and not to another app they work very decently. Most wireless earbuds I tried in the past always had this problem of synchronizing to games. I am absolutely blown away at the quality of these for the price. The biggest downside I have heard is they only last about a year before they stop being functional. But still, for only $20 these are unbeatable if you're on a budget. I recommend the a20i over the p20i as the bass is a little too loud, but you can easily fix this by downloading the soundcore app and choosing the "reduce bass" EQ setting. Also, these are bluetooth only. They don't utilize a USB dongle. So if you have an older laptop or desktop that doesn't have bluetooth, you will need to purchase either a bluetooth dongle or a bluetooth/wifi PCI-E card for your desktop. Anker makes really good stuff all the time. For a really good price no less. They are a real bang-for-buck type company in the best way meaning possible. These earbuds are genuinely very impressive. I d say they are most definitely better than Airpod gen 1 s. The cups are excellent and cut off any outside noise. The bass is very smooth for this price point, the vocals are clear, maybe the treble is a little overpowered by the bass, but besides the bigger charging case and slightly bigger appearance and fit, these are superior to gen 1 airpods for sure and for $20 on sale you literally cannot beat this with any other option. The charge hold time is excellent too and the case charges fast.

text: I m really impressed with these Soundcore P30i earbuds by Anker. The sound quality is very good for the price, especially the bass. Music, videos, and phone calls all sound clear, and the noise cancelling works better than I expected for earbuds in this price range. The battery life is one of the best parts. They last a long time between charges, and the charging case gives plenty of extra playtime. I also like the case design because it doubles as a small phone stand, which is a clever little feature that actually comes in handy when watching videos. They connect quickly with Bluetooth, feel comfortable in the ears, and the app gives you extra control over the sound settings. The IP54 rating is also nice for everyday use, workouts, or being outside. Overall, these are a great value. They sound good, the noise cancelling is solid, the battery life is strong, and they feel like a more expensive pair of earbuds than they actually are. Definitely worth buying. For pure sound quality, I recommend the Moondrop Space Travel TWS Earphone Bluetooth 5.3 Noise Canceling True Wireless Stereo IEMs(Black) over these. They are an off-brand, but popular, semi in ear monitor quality earbud. However, these Soundcore P30i are a lot easier to set up, change modes, an use, while not giving up much in terms of everyday music and video quality. Especially when watching tv and movies, you won t likely notice any difference. So, let s start with a strong average rating for these earbuds. No bad habits. No annoying faults. No weird emphasis on one part of the sound spectrum. How is set up? Well, be prepared for NO pairing button on the case or inside the hood. These enter paring mode automatically the first time you pull them out and remove the little plastic tabs off the charging spots. They will automatically pair with the most recently paired device IF it is on. Otherwise they go into pairing mode. If you really want to move them to another device, please delete from your old device first so you know what s going on. (No probblem if your phone is stolen and you want to re-pair to your new phone). There is another step that I recommend. Download the Soundcore app. Use it to customize your settings. Especially you need to deal with ANC - activve noise cancelling. Do you want it On,

text: want to re-pair to your new phone). There is another step that I recommend. Download the Soundcore app. Use it to customize your settings. Especially you need to deal with ANC - activve noise cancelling. Do you want it On, Off, or Automatic? And set up the desired right and left ear taps for usual stuff like activating a voice assistant or answering a call. THE AUTOMATIC ON-OFF FOR ANC IS SOMETHING I HAVEN T SEEN BEFORE. It doesn t activate in my apartment even when noisy trucks go by. I assume if activates automatically on airplanes, where the engine and airstream noise are constant (other reviewers have flown and confirm this. HOWEVER, given the new menu on the control panel, make sure your earbuds are in Automatic mode; when these first shipped (I have early release and current release firmware versions) they were Automatic only, with no way to force ANC as with other ANC earbuds. I think these should ship in Automatic mode, but they seem to ship in ANC-off mode now; use the app to select Auto mode, and to set the taps you want to use to cycle through the three ANC, Normal (silicone tips block some external sounds), and Transparent modes (Transparent uses the mics to relay external sounds through to your ears, as though you don t have silicone noise blocking tips in the ear canal). Finally, the current firmware adds a chime to the three modes as you cycle through them. I would like to see a set of XL for extra large ear tips. Apple has started doing this. It is a challenge for some earbud designs since the cup holding the tip would need to be larger so the earbuds fit, but then the stem with the charging pads might wobble. Apple and Beats seem to be the only makers with the XL option. With L tips on, I have a good, but not snug, fit. On other earbuds with ANC test modes, I need noticeably snug earbud tips or I don t bass the onboard ANC test. I intend to keep the app on my device for my next flight and test out Automatic and forced ANC modes and see if there is any difference. Also, to compare to the original Beats (not the later Plus) version) earbuds. I don t need high fidelity inflight, just enough ANC so I can actually hear dialogue over the cabin roar. text: I m an audiophile, not an expert, but i enjoy it as a hobby, these earbuds are comparable to those name-brand wired studio buds, but about $500 less and WIRELESS. They fit good and come with extra rubber tips of different sizing for a variety of different ear shapes. When i first got these i was amazed, every song in my playlist is like listening to a totally NEW song, the clarity is insane, you can hear pretty much all the bass notes, and does NOT distort at all(i listen to mainly Gojira, SUPER HEAVY) and any other WIRELESS earbuds struggle to compete with this kind of clarity, i also have a set of airpod pros and they sound cheap compared the these. the only thing apple beats is the noise canceling, Status is still really really good, but apple just does it. The battery is also incredible, i use these for work and mainly just while i m at home and i can go a week without charging them using them for a few hours daily. One small issue, about 3 months in to use the right earbud stopped working all together, when i put it in my ear on transparency it felt like it was on noise canceling and would still play music, but stupid quiet, like not even usable, so i reached out to status about my issue and they responded with surprising speed with options to hard reset and to email them back with the results, unfortunately the reset did not fix the earbud so i emailed them and they literally got me a shipping confirmation same day and they work great. been using for about 4 months since i got the replacement and no further issues, I LOVE YOU STATUS. I can proudly say i do not use my airpods anymore. I just got these today and let me start of by saying.... DAMN!!! I was using Anker's Soundcore Life P3s for a CO pleasure years till they just got old and worn out. I loved those earbuds. I was trying to decide between these and Sony WF-1000XM5s. I wanted a set of earbuds that has good bass but not muddy. Everyone was saying the Sony's were good for bass but muddy. Everyone was saying the 3ANC were damn good and cost less

text: I rarely do reviews but I replaced a very expensive pair of earbuds with these and the sound quality is ridiculously good. The best $16 I've spent in quite some time. Very good fit for my ears, only the sun mates brand also fit, all others fall out even with various sized pod cushions. Also a neat feature is on my various podcasts I can start and pause media we present on stream. Highly recommend very affordable very easy to use ... Haven t had them very long so I can t speak to how long they last, but they are excellent quality for such a cheap price. I personally think the touch to pause is annoying, but could be helpful in some situations. Sound quality is good, I get great sounds for podcast, metal, and rock. Even at a low volume I can drown out the bus and subway noises. I especially like that they re cheap so when I inevitably lose one I can justify buying a new pair. My only complaint is that I have very small ears and these seem to force my ears open a little more than other ear buds so it starts to hurt my ears after around 20-30 minutes, but that s a minor inconvenience for the price and quality. Bater life has also so far lasted multiple hours and haven t had them die yet.

text: These Sony earbuds are my first truly wireless earbuds. For the price they are a perfect entry point. I bought these so that I could have noise cancellation when operating loud equipment for my job that doesn't require true hearing protection. The noise cancellation works well in most situations, it is able to take the noise of a loud high school gym down to a whisper and completely eliminate ambient noise in a room. The buds also include an ambient noise mode that plays the audio around you with no noticable delay. Great for listening to music while still being aware of what's around you or if someone needs to get your attention. The earbuds fit well and come with several different sizes based on your ear canal. Make sure you figure out the right size for you to make the seal better for noise cancelling. The tap controls function smoothly and are slightly configurable from the Sony app. Bluetooth connection was simple once the app is installed, the app also allows you to update the software of the earbuds. The battery life is sufficient for daily use and the case has plenty of extra charge. It does not take long to charge the earbuds. The construction of the earbuds seems relatively durable. Visually they look a lot nicer than airpods and many other wireless earbuds. The case however feels cheap and plastic. One thing to watch out for is not getting the beds positioned on the charging prongs of the case. This will cause the bud that is not connected to stay on and drain it's battery. Overall, a great low cost pair of earbuds that would be good for someone just getting into wireless noise cancelling buds or someone looking to save a dime over flashier more expensive brand options. Quite good! I had been using a pair of wf-1000xm4s previously that I enjoyed, but they were getting long in the tooth so I was going to jump to a different brand as I wasnt happy with the shape of the xm6s. I happened to see these and looked into them and was quite happy with how they seemed to perform in testing. After trying them out I'm happy to report they fit my ears way better then the xm4s and sound just as good if not better then them! They also seem to be a lot louder which is not bad or good for me but if you are hard of hearing they should be good for that. The anc is also very good, I haven't been able to use them
product_name: Sony WF-C710N Truly Wireless Noise-Canceling Earbuds with up to 30-Hour Battery Life, Lightweight and Secure, Black

text: not better then them! They also seem to be a lot louder which is not bad or good for me but if you are hard of hearing they should be good for that. The anc is also very good, I haven't been able to use them too much yet but they seem to block the shop noise at my workplace better then the xm4s so no complaints there. Did have a little trouble getting them connected to my phone but once I did they havent had any issues reconnecting. Over all, a great buy at the retail price and even better for the 56$ I got a refurbished pair for! Overall, these earbuds are excellent, and they are an amazing value compared to other options out there. The biggest issue I have is the gestures. I don't find one tap play/pause to be useful, since they have in-ear detection tied to that already. What I do wish was more convenient is gestures for changing the volume, but some madman decided the best gesture for that is to frantically tap one ear or the other repeatedly. IT TAKES 4 REPEATED TAPS for one single up or down. And they offer some configuration, but it is severely limited. Just two themes - playback or cancellation. Please, give us the options from Inzone Buds! My only other minor gripe is that the plastic is very smooth and slippery, so sometimes they can be hard to grip without dropping. A matte texture instead of gloss would be better. Other than that, I love them. The battery life is amazing. In my use, it has been better than what they claim. They are missing some of the fancy features from their more expensive earbuds, but the tradeoff is better battery life. I'll gladly take that! The noise cancellation is quite good, as is the sound in general. I'm happy with them listening to music or movies. I haven't ever used them for any calls, so I don't know how well the mic works. They fit in my ears quite well, and they come with a few different tips for different size ears. The main piece just rests inside the outer part of your ear and is held in by the rubber tip. I haven't tried, but I don't think these would work well for activities like running, as they don't have anything else holding them in place. When stationary or walking, I have had no issues. They also sit flush enough for me that I

text: Rugged and Durable: Built with military-grade impact resistance and IP67 waterproof rating, the Impact Earbuds are designed to withstand the harshest conditions, making them ideal for outdoor adventures like hiking, rock climbing, and more. Long-Lasting Battery: Boasting a remarkable 90 hours of total playtime and a 12-hour battery life per charge, these earbuds will fuel your grind for days on end without the need for frequent recharging. Liberating Sound Experience: With active noise cancellation, a wide frequency range of 20-20,000Hz, and touch controls, the Impact Earbuds deliver an immersive and customizable audio experience, allowing you to enjoy your music without distractions. RESETTING YOUR EARBUDS - Delete The Impact Earbuds from the bluetooth settings on your device, and place the earbuds back in its case. While earbuds are in the capsule, hold the center Reset button for 5 seconds or until the capsule LED flashes yellow 3 times. Close the capsule to complete the reset function. EARBUDS WILL NOT CHARGE - Ensure the charging case is charged, indicated by LED on the front: should be an amber or green color. Confirm there is nothing blocking the earbuds from the connection to the case. Insert earbuds into the case, and confirm a blinking amber LED on the front of the case, which indicates that the earbuds are charging.

text: At time of writing Ive had these headphones about a year. These are my first set of TWS (true wireless stereo) headphones. I was hesitant to get this type of headphones thinking I would easily lose the earbud, but so far I have not. They do have the ability to play a chime if you are trying to find a misplaced earbud, which is nice (ive used this more than once) I find them to be very comfortable to wear, though I do get a bit of fatigue after a few hours and have to take them out for a bit. They stay pretty well put even when active (working out, cycling, etc) The noise cancelling is great. Cuts out the low frequency drone on planes and trains, background chatter at airports, etc; even without music playing. You do still hear people talking to you, but it muffles it significantly...if someone is trying to talk to me with the headphones in listening to music, it takes a minute for me to realize it. Wind buffeting is usually pretty minimal; I wear these headphones cycling and its usually not noticeable amount of buffeting. Im not an audiophile, but I think the sound quality is very good, and has good lows/bass for an earbud. Battery life is excellent. You can get probably 8 hours straight out of the headphones, but since the case charges them, if you take them out and put them in the case for a bit they will top off. I dont normally wear them until the battery dies, but Id say you can probably get 3 or 4 full charges from the case. Recharging the headphones is quick, and recharging the case itself is also quick (USB C). The earbuds self-locate (magnetically) into the case, so they quickly stow away and also dont just fall out when you open the case. The case has 3 led lights that indicate charge level of the case. The case lid feels a little flimsy, but so far it has held up fine. I disabled the tap actions of the headphones, but it does have the functionality and are customizable in the app. The app is pretty good and easy to use. With my iPhone, sometimes when i answer a call it does not use the headphones and i have to manually select that which is kind of annoying but not a deal breaker. Im sure the AirPods would be better integrated for answering calls. Not sure if they were on
product_name: JBL Live Pro 2 - True Wireless Noise Cancelling Earbuds, up to 40 Hours of Playback with Wireless Charging, 6 mics for Perfect Calls, Touch & Voice Control, IPX5 Water Resistant (Black)

The JBL app is one of the best earbud companion apps I've used. It blows the BEATS app out of the water and is much better than the majority of its competition. text: I am not a paid reviewer. These things work great. I have a Motorola edge phone and these ear buds connected immediately. Didn't have to do anything! Just hit the pair button. There isn't any paper work so I'm trying to figure out the buttons on the ears bud still but the sound quality is great. Definitely noise cancelling without a problem. And they're comfy. They don't hurt my ears at all. I got the olive green paid and I love the color. Beautiful. Definitely worth what i paid. See Update at end, no more pain: Almost all reviews have no problem with the fit of these earbuds, but for me, they hurt. Not unbearably so, but it would be nice to not feel any discomfort at all. I tried changing the tips, adjusting the angle in my ear and also tried leaving loosely outside of ear canal, but always have noticeable discomfort. Plus if I leave them loose, I lose sound quality, so I don't like doing that. Would like to take away two stars but only removing one because these are really good earbuds otherwise for the price. I have never run out of power. Usually when I recharge the case, it doesn't even need recharging. I just do it to go back up to 100%. Despite the fact that I can feel them, it's not excruciating pain, so I do use them often. I just have to keep playing around with other tips and other means of making them more comfortable for me. The case is very attractive. In fact, someone saw my case with buds and commented, "Wow, those are nice!" I don't mind the size. I think it might be the reason the charge lasts so long. If you cut a deck of cards in half and placed on top of each other, that's about the size of the case. The earbuds themselves are attractive but also bulky. Maybe all earhook buds are bulky, I don' t know as these are my first. I wish they had skintone colors to make them less noticeable. I ordered rose gold thinking they would be less noticeable, but should've just gone with black. The sound quality, to me, is exceptional. I use for listening to music, to lessons, and have used for TV. I have not used for phone calls because I rarely use these thru my phone. I have owned these for about 3 months now and am still getting used to
product_name: Wireless Earbuds 75hrs Bluetooth 5.4 Headphone Sport, 2026 Bluetooth Earbuds Stereo Deep Bass Over Ear Bud with Earhooks, ENC Noise Cancelling Mic, IPX7 Waterproof Earphone for Gym/Workout, Royal Blue

text: exceptional. I use for listening to music, to lessons, and have used for TV. I have not used for phone calls because I rarely use these thru my phone. I have owned these for about 3 months now and am still getting used to the different button taps. I'm used to power, pair, pause, fast forward and back, but have to practice a little more on volume up and down. So there you have it. If they didn't hurt a little and the earbuds weren't so bulky, they'd be perfect. Update: I finally figured out how to stop the pain which was getting really bad to point where I felt like I was getting a sore. I put them on one day without looking and later realized no more pain. What I didn't realize was that although I had placed the buds firmly in my ear, the earhooks were not hooked behind my ear. Instead they were just resting on my ear. I'll try to add a picture later. Even though not hooked behind my ear, they were still secure with absolutely no discomfort. Who knew?!! The other thing is I was streaming music on my phone for hours at work and walked away thinking the phone was in my pocket. Very large building and I went very far away. Phone was not in my pocket but the music never stopped. And last thing, I said I don't use for phone calls, but another time while streaming music, a call came in. The music stopped for the call. It was seamless. I was at home and went into a different room away from phone and could still have the conversation. Changing to 5 stars Last update: Owned these almost a year now. I do hook behind my ear nowadays without discomfort. The hooks are very bendy and stretchy for adjusting fit. The sound is really good and rich even when using only one bud at work. These are some of the best budget earbuds I have ever used. They are very comfortable, my ears are often sensitive to ear buds but these are not painful. The sound quality is exceptional, fantastic bass, and listening to music is excellent. But perhaps the best feature is the battery life, I have to charge the holder maybe once a week, and I use these earbuds at least 6 hours a day. They do not feel cheap, and Ive dropped this while biking and theres zero damage to my case.

text: Comfortable and easy to wear for hours without my ears hurting. Sony has nailed the design for long term wear for all wireless Bluetooth earbuds I've owned. Does not fall out easily and fits ergonomically. I am confident I won't lose these easily. I love the large button pressed to control music, calls, etc. I enjoy that it's not touch-sensitive like Samsung earbuds, which have been falsely set off by my hair or beanie. My complaints: these buttons are not programmable: what type of madman want people to press 4 times to change volume up/down? I would also love a "Find Earbuds" function if they are connected to a device, but the owner isn't sure where they placed them. Noise cancellation is non-existent, but you have ambient sound (listen to your surroundings) if you wish to have both music and some situational awareness. The case is *slim*, much slimmer than Sony's previous cases. Sound is very decent for the price. With the latest iterations of Bluetooth, these earbuds can be connected to 2 devices at a time, but they cannot play sound from both sources at once. All in all, I recommend if you need a decent and comfortable pair of earbuds with which to listen to music and audio for hours. Sometimes, I use them for more than 6 hours and I'm surprised the battery still goes strong. I've been using them for 5 months now, and like other Sony earbuds I've owned in the past, I expect their longevity to last years. I love these earbuds. Fit perfectly and comfortably in my ear. Lightweight so I've forgotten I've even had them in. Long lasting battery life. Very very durable!! Settings are easy to adjust, they work great from a long range. The sound and hearing ability is perfect. No background noise perfect clarity. Definitely great value. Design is great. highly recommend these earbuds. The Sony WF C510 earbuds have been an excellent addition to my daily routine. The sound is clean and balanced, the multipoint connection works smoothly when switching between devices, and the mic performs well for calls. They re lightweight, comfortable, and the IPX4 water resistance makes them reliable for workouts or Florida humidity. The battery life easily lasts through the day, and the charging case keeps them powered without worry. My order was well packaged and arrived within the intended timeframe,
