text: I am very happy with my JBL TUNE headphones! Once charged they have incredible battery life and they are comfortable to wear more so than my ear buds. In my opinion. As far as the quality of sound you can get better but will spend more money doing so. I am satisfied with the audio quality and the different settings like noise canceling and ambient aware. Worth the money I spent on them. The app you download on your phone is disappointing. Not much there. I am happy with my purchase. Good balance of affordability and quality, easy connectivity, and good value for the money I spent. So far so good. I received the head phones in a timely manner. The box was smaller than I anticipated but housed the headset, the charging cable, a 1/8'" cable and the instructions. I turned on the Bluetooth on my computer, powered up the headset, hit the sync button and it connected automatically. The buttons are raised and easy to use. The volume buttons are pronounced and easy to operate. The headset did start to feel heavy after about an hour of use and the range of the Bluetooth is limited to about 15 foot. I may have had some interference with the microwave. The sound is very good with pronounced lows and clear highs. I didn't have to adjust the volume too much from noise discomfort. They did a good job at drowning out background noise. I would recommend them for the price. This is my first pair of noise canceling headphones. I really like these so far. Used them at work all day and barely used 20% of the battery. The sound quality is great. Haven't used them for calls yet so I can't say on the mic quality. They are pretty comfortable, but I do wish the ear cups were slightly larger. The quality is good and the controls on the headphones work great. It also has a companion app to download to customize the EQ and voice assist functions. Overall I would def recommend these as a great entry level noise canceling headphone. For the price they are a great buy for under 100 dollars. I will definitely recommend them to all my friends and family looking for a good pair of headphones on a budget.

text: My audio background is "sweet spot" grade audio. Not cheap, not placebo tier, but Goldilocks zone. Think Superlux 668B, SHP9500, Galaxy Buds 3 Pro, Galaxy buds 2, an old Sennheiser BT 4.40 and so on. This falls exactly into that price zone and it absolutely crushes all of them on the thing I care about the most: clarity. TLDR if you need THE BEST noise canceling in this price bracket, get this. I cannot stand muddy or compressed sounding audio like the Sennheiser had. I like being able to hear every layer of audio clearly and this accomplishes that. Comfort wise you'll forget you're wearing these for an hour or so. These are the most comfortable closed backs that I have (not as good as the open backs+ velour pads I have, but it is what it is if you want good isolation). If there's one nitpick I have is that the clamping force is too weak for you to lay down with it unless you have a big head. It'll just slide right off your ears. I use these primarily to help me deal with my sound sensitivity post TBI. The sound of mowers in my neighborhood was so painful that I had to put on Loops and headphones just to try to block it. This will do it all on its own perfectly! Galaxy Buds of any variety I had couldn't stop it, but this sure can. Mic quality: this is totally sufficient for work and phone calls when indoors. Haven't tried outside yet but Sound Guys have a demo and it's not great for that. There's a 3.5mm jack on it so I can connect it to my desktop with my Vmoda Boom Pro for a better mic and a wired connection. Software is a little weird. I don't know why they bothered to make their own janky and poor AI assistant, but that's off by default: it'll use your phone's one instead. I thought I'd have to fiddle with EQ to get it to sound good, but the default tuning is actually my favorite through my testing. I got this on launch day because I happened to need a solution to the mower noise RIGHT NOW but I bet there will be some sweet sales frequently knowing the brand, but I thought it was worth it at full price, even as miserly as I am. It comes with a short type
product_name: Soundcore Space 2 by Anker, Active Noise Cancelling Headphones, Comfortable Fit, 50H Playtime, LDAC Hi-Res Wireless Audio, Dual Connection, Clear Calls, Bluetooth 6.1, Nap Mode, Wearing Detection

text: happened to need a solution to the mower noise RIGHT NOW but I bet there will be some sweet sales frequently knowing the brand, but I thought it was worth it at full price, even as miserly as I am. It comes with a short type C cable (you already have a bazillion, here's one more) and a short super thin 3.5mm cable. Maybe ok for connecting to your laptop. Multi point works great, which was my #1 pet peeve with the Galaxy Buds series is that they DIDN'T do that. It's basically seamless on here and I just don't think about switching. I now own both this pair and the space one, not the space one pro. For the price, this is an upgrade on every single feature you can think of over the space one. I tried both headphones side by side today, comparing them to the same track with the same EQ, and the winner is pretty clear to me. Space two has bigger soundstage, better imaging, noise cancellation is slightly better, however I was pretty impressed with how good the NC is on the original Space one which is really an ultra budget NC headphone right now. For only $120, plus a free case right now, you really can't do no wrong, this is probably the budget king of NC headphone on the market right now. And one thing it does above all else, is the comfort. One thing I hated about over the head headphones is that most of are very uncomfortable, this is not true for this fresh pair. It is indeed as advertised, extremely comfortable, although the materials feels no different to the touch on the Space one, the Space two just feels better when wearing for a longer period of time. It's also a bit heavier than the Space one, but I assume that's because the drivers are slightly better on the Space two. Why do I take a star off? Because despite the pros, this pair still sound like a budget pair of headphone, no surprise here since you get what you pay for. The NC sound is extremely modified to the point that Bass is still very forward and feels uncomfortable, even with hearID tuned EQ. It does not sound like a washed down premium sounded headphone, instead it does sound like a pair of very well dressed budget headphone. So if you are expecting Bose or Sony XM level of sound for only $120, look elsewhere. This is for people with a budget mindset that wants respectable NC performance and extreme comfort

I just received the new Soundcore Space 2 on launch day and I m already impressed. Here are a few things I ve noticed right out of the box: The Launch Bonus: Just a heads up for everyone I purchased these during the launch window and they came with a FREE hardshell travel case (the grey textured one in my photos). To be clear, the retail box normally includes a black soft carry pouch, so getting the hard case as an extra gift was a fantastic value. It feels very sturdy and fits the headphones perfectly. Weight & Comfort: These are incredibly lightweight (261g). I ve been wearing them while setting up and they feel like they re barely there. The memory foam is soft and doesn t feel like it s clamping too hard on my head. Setup & Firmware: As soon as I paired them with my iPad and opened the Soundcore app, a firmware update popped up. It took about 5 10 minutes to install, but it really seemed to stabilize the connection. Noise Cancellation (ANC 3.0): The Adaptive ANC is very solid. It easily handles the low hum of my apartment. For sharper sounds like the TV, I found that switching to "Manual Mode - Level 5" in the app works best to really seal out the world. Battery for Days: The box confirms 70 hours of battery (50 with ANC), which is class-leading for this price. Pro-Tip: If you re using these for gaming or movies, make sure to enable the "Dual Device Connection" in the settings. I have mine hooked up to my iPad and my laptop simultaneously and the switching is seamless. Overall, for $129, getting these headphones plus the bonus hardshell case is a steal. Highly recommended!

text: I have no complaints about these headphones. They took me by surprise, I was expecting them to perform closer to their price point. I've purchased 2 sets and both me and my fiance love them! I have a smaller head (I can wear child sized hats and glasses) and my fiance is 6'2 with a big head, we both can wear them for hours and they are VERY comfortable. They feel light on your head, but don't feel like cheap crap. The sound quality is great and I like the "transparency" mode. You can hear whats going on around you if you don't want to pull your headphones off every 5 seconds. The Bluetooth connection is extremely stable and doesn't start cutting out randomly if you are 10-20 feet from your phone, inside and outside. The headset for phone calls is clear and everyone I have called says they can hear me really well. The battery lasts forever. I am able to go 3-4 days without charging them. These do not fold, so I would recommend a place to hang them or a case. I haven't bothered and they seem to hold up fine laying on different surfaces. They work ok as noise canceling headphones as well, I just wouldn't expect silence. I don't use them for their noise canceling function though. The "ANC mode" does help mask other sounds, but if nothing is going through the speakers, you hear some white noise. Good quality headset! It feels very solid. The sound quality is very good. Noice cancellation is good. When I have my music on I cannot hear anything around me. The only thing I don t like is that the volume adjustment on the headset often advances the track, but if you turn the volume down on your device first, you don t need to. It is a good purchase for the price. It feels expensive. Bluetooth connectivity is good. I recommend this headset. text: THE BEST NOISE CANCELLATION: Powered by advanced processors and an adaptive microphone system, the WH-1000XM6 headphones deliver real-time noise cancellation for an immersive, distraction-free listening experience. CO-CREATED WITH MASTERING AUDIO ENGINEERS: Developed in collaboration with world-renowned mastering audio engineers, these headphones deliver unparalleled sound clarity and precision. A specially designed driver with a lightweight carbon fiber dome delivers high fidelity sound, where rich vocals and every instrument remains pure and balanced. Optimized for advanced noise cancellation, the WH-1000XM6 headphones keep every frequency crisp and true to the artist s intent. text: Kinda expensive but well worth the price, you cannot hear anything in them, weither that be someone screaming at you or a blowdryer going off. The sound quality is also unreal, you can bop to this all day and it still hits the next time you put it on. Also the battery life is quite good, I barley have to charge it even after 3-4 days of heavy use. A lot better then my previous Hesh ANC headphones. Also while it isn't mentioned anywhere it is quite water resistant, I wash dogs for a living and they tend to shake and get you soaked and they have'nt rusted or degraded at all, at least from what I can see. Also unlike my previous headphones it has not pulled out my hair a single time when adjusting it, but that makes sense for $500 dollar headphones, the middle section bends quite a lot to but puts little force on your head so it remains quite comfortable for different sized heads. Also unlike my previous headphones again it never interupts you music to say low battery, it only does that once at 10% and lowers your music a tad to say that then ramps the volume back up again. I will say however I kinda like the physical buttons of my previous headphones a lot more then the swipe controls, even though they do not fail as often as you might think physical buttons just, never fail, its annoying when you go to up the volume and you skip the song thats all. It is a buy for me, and I would do it again, the ANC is just to good, you feel like your in a library all the time, even if someone is leafblowing right next to you, it is actually unreal. I purchased the Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones from Amazon for $298.00 about two months ago as an upgrade to the WH-1000XM5 headphones, and I use them regularly. First and foremost, the Active Noise Cancelation (ANC) on these new headphones is the best in its class. With an array of 12 microphones, they capture and cancel-out most frequencies of background noise. It's a nice improvement over the older XM5 model. The sound quality is impressive. The frequencies are well-balanced with regard to bass, midrange and treble. They offer decent bass without being overbearing. They are lightweight and feel comfortable to wear, although they have small earcups which might not fit entirely over the ear
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: The frequencies are well-balanced with regard to bass, midrange and treble. They offer decent bass without being overbearing. They are lightweight and feel comfortable to wear, although they have small earcups which might not fit entirely over the ear. They also have a tight clamping force on the head, which probably loosens over time. This close fit could make the headphones feel uncomfortable if you wear eyeglasses, like I do. The battery life is decent, and they recharge quickly. These headphones offer a plethora of useful features like a transparency mode to hear external sounds and very clear phone calls. In summary, the Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones are expensive and, depending on your individual taste, may or may not be worth the price. I gave them a borderline 5 star rating. My opinion is that if stellar ANC and great sound quality are your objectives, then these headphones might be right for you. If so, make sure that you purchase them while on sale because the regular price is way too expensive. I have a headphone problem, or so my wife says. I ve owned the Apple AirPods Max, the Sony XM5 s, the Bose QC and QC Ultra the QC 45 s and 35 s both I and II, the Sonos Ace, and the Beats Studio Pro. Going into my XM6 purchase, my favorite are/were the Bose QuietComfort Ultra. I bought the QuietComfort Ultra during Prime Day for an unbelievable $329! I have found that they offer the best combination of comfort, sound and ANC for the price. So, how does the XM6 compare? The ANC is second to none. I augment our central air with a window unit, which sits right behind my head, and I can completely eliminate its sound. But static sounds are pretty easily muffled by most of newer ANC headphones, so how about dynamic sounds like a tv or normal conversation? The XM6 excel there, too. Put simply, they offer the best ANC of any of the aforementioned headphones. They are also pretty comfortable and clamp down relatively tight, though I haven t found anything to be as comfortable as either the QC or QC Ultra. The XM6 are designed to be over ear headphones, yet my ear doesn t seem to fit inside the cup like other over ear headphones. This, in my opinion, makes them less-comfortable then they could/should be. However, the memory foam is a nice touch and does help.
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: , yet my ear doesn t seem to fit inside the cup like other over ear headphones. This, in my opinion, makes them less-comfortable then they could/should be. However, the memory foam is a nice touch and does help. The sound is well-balanced and pretty good right out of the box, though I had to make use of the ten-band EQ to get them to sound just a tad better than my QC. With my current EQ settings, I would say they offer the clarity of the Apple AirPods Max, but with the bottom end of my Bose QuietComfort. The bass isn t overpowering, it s actually pretty accurate (after EQ tinkering). In the final analysis, the XM6 offer the best sound of any of the headphones I mentioned, but not by much. Furthermore, the ANC is superior to everything else I ve owned, but not by a staggering amount. So, which should you buy? If you re like me and in search of the best possible sound, and won t settle for anything less than the best ANC, then get the XM6. If, however, you prioritize comfort and value, and don t mind making some very minor concessions regarding sound and ANC, then get the Bose QuietComfort or the QC Ultra (wait until you catch them on sale, which is pretty often). I hope that helps. Happy listening! Update: 6/02/2025: Intermittent crackling in left speaker. Had to return them. I have replaced them, though. Update 06/07/25: I received the replacement pair and everything seems great. I neglected to mention in my previous review how great the Sony app is and how customizable the XM6 are. First, you get a ten band EQ, instead of the Bose three bands. Additionally, there are some awesome (optional) features that are missing from Bose. Sony has speak to chat, which mutes your music and puts your headphones in aware mode when you speak. Also, there is an option to accept or reject calls by simply nodding or shaking your head, which is very cool. If you re into features like that and general customization, then the Sony XM6 are for you. UPDATE 06/17/2025. Started hearing crackling again in the right speaker this time. This time I did a Google search to see if others were having the same problem, and it seems like turning on DSEE Extreme could be the
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: UPDATE 06/17/2025. Started hearing crackling again in the right speaker this time. This time I did a Google search to see if others were having the same problem, and it seems like turning on DSEE Extreme could be the culprit at high volumes. The DSEE Extreme setting is supposed to upscale compressed files. I turned off the setting and no more crackling. I replayed the same part of the same song with and without it engaged, it it s definitely the DSEE Extreme. Consequently, I ve turned off that setting and now everything seems fine.
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: The quality of Razer products astounds to me with how great they are for their prices. When shopping for a new headset, I had little to no prior knowledge to understanding which gaming brands were good for my money. I bought these with the intent of having headphones with a microphone that I could mute with the click of a button and last me a while. I was quite skeptical at first, but this exceeded my expectations. I d say their headsets fall between the medium for its price, but I also say they go beyond for its quality. I ve gotten these a while ago, having kept up with them since 2024. These can rest on your head and ears comfortably and do not cause any discomfort even when worn for prolonged periods of time. The quality of sound and its output is great. It is not underwhelming at all, having a scroll wheel to adjust the volume directly from your headset. Along with its input, the microphone is amazing. I frequently talk using the microphone attached with no concern for bad or choppy quality, I ve seen some reviews with some mixed feelings about the plastic which the headsets use, having its quality compromised for it to be light weight. I have found no issues with the material used and found them comfortable to use long term. These are definitely worth the purchase with their outstanding performance, long-lasting comfortability and reasonable price point. While others may be concerned for parts of its build, my experience with it has been entirely positive. I would recommend these to anybody looking for a solid and lightweight headset for gaming! These are wired over-ear headphones that focus more on passive noise isolation than active noise canceling. The ear cups fit snugly around the ears, which helps reduce outside noise without any electronics involved. This makes them useful in situations where you don t want to worry about batteries or charging. Sound quality is solid for everyday listening. Bass is present but not overpowering, mids are clear, and vocals come through cleanly. They perform well for watching videos, casual music listening, and computer use. The wired connection is stable with no lag, which is helpful for video editing or streaming. Comfort is good for extended use. Padding is soft enough that it doesn t cause pressure quickly, though long sessions may still require short breaks. Build quality feels decent for the price, with adjustable sizing that accommodates different head shapes. Overall, these are dependable wired headphones that do what they re supposed to do without unnecessary features. Theres no need

text: I am a hardcore gamer, so when I need equipment like keyboards, mice, and or headsets I always go with "Steel Series". While it is name brand and can be expensive, you get your moneys worth. This headset is amazing and very comfortable. It has high-quality sound that can let you hear things you normally couldnt hear through a speaker. The microphone sound is clear and everyone listening to you can hear clearly without any inconvenient noises thay come with some mics. I also find it convenient that the mic is fully retractable as that when I am not using it I can simply tuck it away. Another thing I like is that the mic arm is flexible and can be moved closer to you or further out most others. As for the head frame, there is a cloth piece that spans the width of the headset thaf is adjustable and or removable. This is very convenient and comfortable as wearing headsets (particularly the hard plastic that sits on the head), for long periods of time can hurt or be uncomfortable. Also the ear pieces are made from very soft breathable cloth and they are big enough to fit around the ears instead of sitting on them or the edges. I have had multiple headsets and all of them sat on the edges of my ears and would eventually become uncomfortable but not with this set. The ears on this set literally fits AROUND the ENTIRE ear and instead of MOST of the ear. Other sets you can feel your ears under the edge of the cover but with this one you can not feel them at all. As far as long gaming sessions I have sat in on a four hour session with this set and the comfort was so great that I nearly forgot I was wearing a headset. It also has a large built in on / off button for the microphone as well as a big volume roll button for volume control. Update: I have only had these for less than 4 months with light use and for some reason this earpiece snapped off like butter. HOW TO FIX BROKEN EARPIECE: There is a tiny screw that fell out. Find the black screw and then screw it back in and it will be like brand new again. Original Review: I have been rocking these for a few weeks now and I'm impressed with the sound quality, but it would have been nice to show me how the mute button works, because it's kind of hard to tell if it should be pushed down or not for it to be muted at first.

text: I had a specific need: What's a headset I can recommend to my overseas coworkers (some who mumble with thick accents) that has a VERY clear mic, good headphone sound quality, works as USB-A or old-school headphone miniplug, and all-day comfort... and under $60. We have a winner! I would say this has an EXCELLENT mic (the best I've tested), very good comfort, and very good headphones. I also have Sony studio headphones (no mic), and I am an audiophile who could bore you with the details of why those are better for recording, music, etc. But these Corsairs will give anyone audio listening enjoyment. I'm relatively picky about this, but I do know that for 90% of listeners, these sound good enough to be their only music headphones and they wouldn't tell any difference with my Sony's. Alternatives I looked at: The Logitech H390a is a cheapy, with a very clear, VERY bright (perhaps overly bright for many speakers, but this is a fabulous solution for mumblers) microphone, but stiff headphone pads on the ears. I did a 1-hour call with them, and it was okay... but I wouldn't want them for 3 hours in a row. The SteelSeries Artic Nova 1P - I wanted to love these, comfy, great headphones, but the mic is a little muffled, especially when too close to the mouth. But yeah, nowhere near the mic quality of this one. I dropped a whopping $160 on the Jabra Evolve2 65 - and everyone should know this: 95% of the WIRELESS headsets out there are going to have very poor audio from the bluetooth for the mic. I returned these when I found out. Wanted to love them for their comfort and headphone quality, but the lack of high-frequencies in the mic is a total deal breaker for me. If you want the most UNDERSTANDABLE audio quality for work calls, this Corsair set is the best I've found! Better than the apple wired set. (which was my previous gold standard, but certainly picks up way more environmental noise.) Could no longer put them on my head, sounded great and looked very similar to these but quality and durability was rather poor, these are perfect, they looked a lot like the old pair I had with a few minor differences, but when I looked closer and ran my fingers across the joints, turns out the joints are the same shape as my old ones, but looks like Corsair reinforced them with some metal, the joints you can tell are metal cause they have a glossy texture, not like matt plastic, and they get colder when you let them sit like metal does, just seeing this little detail was like icing on the cake, not to metion for a chat mic, the clarity is unbelievable as are the phones, nice ballanced tone for listening to music and 7.1 virtual surround is wonderful for gaming, as it has an advantage to pick up on directional audio in video games very nicely. Pair it with Dolby Atmos and it's even better too. Highly recommend these headphones, I managed to pick up my pair on sale, for me worth every penny. Ok, the MSRP of this headphones is 70 USD, but is common to see in 50 or even 40, so if you bought this for that price is excellent, is not better gaming headset under 50 USD, has a really good sound quality and the micro is outstading, maybe i never gonna have another headset with this micro quality, is way too much micro that the hyperx cloud III (no hate, only a comparison point) and feels really confortable even for people with big heads and presciption glasses like me and the auto EQ Sound ID is amazing, really gives you a sound for your taste, so anything looks perfect, yep, except for the quality construncion. And dont take me wrong, feels really solid, but the ear pads are really bad, you will need change in the future (1>year) due to the bad glue, and that is 15 USD more to the price and also the plastic headband becomes rigid so is easier to broke, also the micro is really good but is some quiet so you will need so increase the gain level.
product_name: Corsair HS65 Surround Multiplatform Wired Gaming Headset Dolby Atmos SonarWorks SoundID iCUE Compatible PC, Mac, PS5, PS4, Xbox, Nintendo Switch, Mobile Carbon

text: (note: I wear glasses. I feel like this is important to mention with headsets >.>) Honestly, I never expected headphones to make such a big difference in experience. My first headset was a 25$-ish headset that broke, so I decided well, lets try something a bit better from a reputable brand, and ended up buying this one for 40$, and it is worth every. single. cent. spent. Audio is amazing, and it comes with an application you can download that allows you to tweak the sound depending on your use. It did seem a bit overwhelming at first, but its actually fairly simple to use once you get to understand it a bit. The headset does feel a little tight on the head, but so far its not uncomfortable to use for long periods of time, as long as you put it on right. Even while using glasses, this feels ok on the head. If you put it on wrong however, like I did when I first got it, it would be very uncomfortable and even painful. My only complaints would be that, because its all plastic and feels a bit hard, I get worried about breaking it and try to use it as gently as I can. However, I have seen a Youtuber do a review of this headset and he stretches it beyond what my heart can handle without breaking, so there's that little bit of relief found there xD. The 2nd complaint is the short wire length. Would have loved having it be a bit longer, but its not bad. Overall, my experience with it has been pretty well. So well that, despite the 2 complaints I listed above, I'd still be willing to give this a 5/5. (edit: 4.5 now, due to below issue) Edit: after using it for a couple months, this headset is amazing and is no longer as tight on the head. However, now I'm facing a new issue, and that is the mic wont fully retract into the headset and would get stuck like there is something blocking it from being pushed into its slot. This comes and goes and I have to use some level of force here and there to get it fully in, but out of fear of breaking it, I stopped doing that. Still, because I only use it for the pc, its not really much of an issue except when I'm eating. For that inconvenience, id drop the review to 4.5. Would still buy
product_name: SteelSeries Arctis Nova 1 Multi-System Gaming Headset Hi-Fi Drivers 360 Spatial Audio Comfort Design Durable Ultra Lightweight Noise-Cancelling Mic PC, PS5/PS4, Switch, Xbox - White

text: of breaking it, I stopped doing that. Still, because I only use it for the pc, its not really much of an issue except when I'm eating. For that inconvenience, id drop the review to 4.5. Would still buy a steelseries though because its a fantastic audio experience. I ve been using the SteelSeries Arctis Nova 1 Multi System headset and honestly it surprised me in a good way. The first thing I noticed was how comfortable it is. The ear cushions are soft and it doesn t clamp too tight, so even after a few hours of gaming or watching videos, my head doesn t feel sore. Sound quality is clear and balanced. Footsteps and small in game details are easy to hear, which actually gives you an advantage in competitive games. Music sounds clean too, not overly bass heavy but not flat either. It feels well tuned for everyday use, not just gaming. The microphone is another strong point. My voice comes through clearly and teammates have not complained about background noise or distortion. For a headset in this price range, the mic quality is better than expected. What I also like is that it works with different systems without any hassle. I can plug it into my PC or console and it just works. No complicated setup. Overall, it feels like a smart buy if you want something comfortable, reliable, and good sounding without spending a crazy amount of money. Theres some pros and cons to this. Comparing to my old headset these were much louder and the noise cancellation were much better. But the mic quality in discord specifically was a bit meh other people i was with said it sounded like i was underwater. I would still recommend this headset as its pretty good for budget, the audio quality was nice and for your ear it was really comfortable. Also the wire was a bit short to my liking. UPDATE AFTER 2 YEARS OF USE : I daily this thing as my main headset and yes there's a few problem already. First of all the headset like leather material keep falling off and currently all of it is gone. Secondly the jack insert bent (this one is my fault but yeah still a problem). Third issue I'm running into is the mic barely working as it sometimes not detecting anything and i mean anything. Another issue is the headset ear puff almost falling out. I mean it has been treated a bit rough but since it lasted 2 years I can say its pretty good

text: WOW! I am a 77-year-old male with no hearing issues. I have played (drums) all sorts of music. I have played around with writing music (notes) and lyrics. I have a natural ear for music. I have heard all sorts of music played on high end turntables, vinyl, recorded, digital sounds/songs, etc., through all sorts of speakers from high end to no-names. I have tried countless headsets. I have spent a small fortune on sound delivery systems, in the form of headsets and speakers. I have heard songs and sounds in sound rooms and in hallways. I was absolutely blown away at this set of headphones. I used them in "dongle" mode, not Bluetooth mode The sharpness, crispness and distinctiveness of the bass to the high notes is impressive. The bass sounds do NOT pound your ear drums! They do not painfully vibrate your middle ear. The separation of chords, instruments (brass, percussion, wind, etc.), and vocals is very impressive. Every element and nature of sound is captured. Yet these headphones manage to bring out the essence of pure, crisp sound. The headphones actually immerse you in what the original sound engineer wanted you to hear and enjoy. Great headset! Comfortable to wear with soft ear cushions that don t cause pressure even after long use. The microphone works well and the sound is clear. Perfect for both work and gaming. Build quality is excellent. 5 stars for this headset! The noise-canceling microphone works very well my teammates can hear me without background noise. I also love being able to switch between wireless and Bluetooth. Battery life is outstanding I ve gone several days of heavy use without needing to recharge. Love it

text: I love this headset! I'm coming from a set of astro a50 gen2's. The astros headband is slightly bigger than these but give me massive headaches :/. These barely fit on my head. So I wish the band had a few more clicks left for adjustment. Anyways sound wise they are decent. Pretty detailed sounding although they lack in the bass department. For reference I use fostex 6xx mahogany's from drop. They don't sound as good as my fostex but they sound better than my Astros. Everything sounds clear without being exaggerated. I love being able to take calls and still have audio from whatever the dongles plugged in to. The microphone blows my astro a50's mic out of the water. I finally sound clear vs sounding like a walkie talkie. Construction is great. Mostly metal and very sturdy. Ear cups feel great although they can get your ears pretty warm. Some more breathable pads may help here. The carrying case is ok. Wish it was a little bigger so putting away the headphones didn't require shoving them in. But it was my choice to use the case. I do appreciate having magnets on the flap for it And the little inner pouch for wires,dongle, and mic. Definitely would pick it up used vs paying full price though. UPDATE: Reached out to Corsair support even though it was out of warranty and they replaced it for free. 10/10 support. Not worth. The battery life after a few months went down to 8-12 hours. Had to replace the ear cuffs 8 months in because they broke off. And I don t do any crazy things while wearing my headset. Most I do is walk down some stairs while wearing it. And 16 months in the microphone stopped working entirely. Replaced the mic. Still doesn t work. All I get is some random intermittent crackling feedback when I move or speak into the mic, even when the headset isnt connected to anything. I had the old model and it was really good. Lasted for years. This one just hasn t been it. The battery life is worse, the range is worse, and the quality of parts.. is worse The Corsair Virtuoso XT is a very well-built gaming headset with some standout strengths. Build quality is exceptional, and it feels premium in hand. The microphone is easily the best I ve used on a gaming headset, comparable to some $60 $70 desk microphones,
product_name: Corsair Virtuoso RGB Wireless XT Multiplatform Gaming Headset with Bluetooth - Dolby Atmos - Broadcast Quality Microphone - iCUE Compatible- PC, Mac, PS5, PS4, Nintendo Switch, Mobile - Black

text: XT is a very well-built gaming headset with some standout strengths. Build quality is exceptional, and it feels premium in hand. The microphone is easily the best I ve used on a gaming headset, comparable to some $60 $70 desk microphones, which is impressive. My main complaint is the stock earpads. They are too small, causing my ears to touch the drivers, which can be uncomfortable. However, the earpads are very easy to swap, so this isn t a major issue. I ended up buying several replacements without any trouble. Out of the box, the default tuning lacks bass and sub-bass, but the Corsair EQ software and Dolby Atmos support work very well. The Dolby Access surround included with the headset is one of the few virtual surround solutions that actually feels useful. In games, latency is very low, and directional audio is excellent you can clearly distinguish footsteps and positioning. They are a bit expensive for what they offer, and the sound quality could be better at full price. That said, if you can find them on sale, they are definitely worth it especially if you value comfort, customization, and microphone quality over pure audiophile sound.

text: I'm writing this review after owning this pair of headphones for the past three years. They're incredibly durable, sound excellent, and have an outstanding battery life. I charge these headphones once a week and I've never even got a low battery alert, and the range on these things is incredible. If I attach the dongle to the top of my PC, I can be two rooms away and still have a clear connection. These headphones are very comfortable and I've worn them for 8+ hours before with no issues. The cups sit over your ears so you don't have to worry about them straining your ears. These are well worth the money, they've been going strong for years and I suspect they'll keep on for many more. This is the headphones you want. Okay, that's kind of a subjective statement, everyone has their own opinions, but they are the headphones for me. I hope them never discontinue them (because I assume they'll eventually wear out). So everyone said the battery lasts forever, that's true. I've had them for 7 months now, I wear them all day at work and I think I've only charged them 3 times (I usually wait for it to get to 30 or 40 percent before charging them). The audio is good and they are plenty loud. I think if you are an audiophile or something you might not care for the audio, but for me as a gamer I think they are fine. The one complaint I have about them is the mic is not fancy or anything, it doesn't extend or fold up all nicely you just curl it up to get it out of your face. The mic also plugs into the headset with a standard audio jack and one time it got knocked lose (not sure how, it fits in nice and snug) and I wasn't broadcasting and I thought it was broken and was super upset, but once I realized I just needed to shove the mic back into the socket everything was find after that, so watch out for that in case you think it's broken. That said, no real complaints. Fairly comfortable, they seem very reliable, they are simple enough that I don't think there are many points of failure so I expect them to last 4-5 years easy (but we'll see). Price is kind of high for a simple headset, so it might be out of reach for some, but I think they are quality and if you are going to take a chance
product_name: HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless - Gaming Headset for PC, 300-hour battery life, DTS Headphone:X Spatial Audio, Memory foam, Dual Chamber Drivers, Noise-canceling mic, Durable aluminum frame,Red

text: Honest review, So I will start off by saying I've had a apple watch series 5 since it came out. Never had a issue with the watch other than battery life wasnt that great. I just got the s26 ultra and watch has just become a digital clock. I needed something that worked with my new phone. After researching tons of watches I came across this gem. I was happy with the price and it had all the features I needed. From what I learned you can't tap to pay with it but I never even used that on my Apple watch but for notifications, tracking my fitness activity and contolling my music. This watch is perfect. You can answer calls, reply to text and stay connected. I can't always have my phone in my hand at work so seeing my notifications are important to me. I got the watch with 56 percent battery life 3 days ago and I'm at 39 percent now. This is the first watch light enough and comfortable enough to even sleep with so I am now also tracking my sleep patterns. I haven't written a review on Amazon in ages but I honestly must say Amazon did a amazing job with this watch. It has so many face watch options and very easy to connect to the phone. If your looking for a fitness tracking good quality watch for a affordable price. I truly highly recommend this watch. I am very happy with my purchase and even if I decide to go back to the apple iPhone. I will still choose to stay with this watch over paying a few hundred dollars for a watch that I have to charge daily and don't use half the features it has. The one thing I'm not sure if this has that I know can come in handy is the sos feature but for 80 bucks I will not complain. Update. I received the watch on March 20 and lost the charger puck right away. Went on a vacation. Watch did not die till April 1st. Ordered a new charger puck that arrives today. I changed all the settings to make it last while out the country but for the watch to last almost 14 days on half battery I will say is amazing. I ve been using the Amazfit Bip 6 Smartwatch for about 14 days now, and one of the first things that really stood out to me is the battery life. After two full weeks of use, my watch is still sitting at 44%, which is honestly impressive. I do typically turn Bluetooth on

Overall Thoughts: The Amazfit Bip 6 is a solid smartwatch for anyone looking for long battery life, useful health tracking features, and customizable options without needing to charge it constantly. text: I've had this watch about 2 weeks and I love it. It's very comfortable to wear. It looks great and it does what I need it to do. I didn't need a fancy smart watch. I was really looking for a fitness tracker. Before this watch I had a Garmin Vivosmart 4 but after 5 years the band broke. I didn't like that the Vivosmart 4 had a very sold part that was built into the silcone band. Once it broke I was unable to repair the band. I like that this watch has a band that can be replaced and I'll probably get a few replacement bands just to be sure this lasts a while. I don't change my tech out very often. I'm using the band with a Samsung Galaxy FE23 phone so it syncs up easily. The accuracy seems decent but I wasn't expecting it to be perfect anyway. I haven't tried out the GPS connection and tracking yet. I do a lot of parade marching in the summer and it'll be getting a good test then. The watch is very easy to charge and charges quickly. The one feature that I kind of wish it had would be contactless payment but that's not a deal breaker for me. One thing I was concerned about with this watch was that it wasn't coming directly from Samsung and it's an international version that doesn't have a US warranty. I bought the Asurion warranty just to be safe. What I got was NOT a used product. It was new and in a sealed box. I took photos as proof. When the watch started up the text was in Spanish but the first thing to do was setting up the language so that was easy to fix. I definitely recommend this watch if you're looking for just a fitness tracker. It's a very good product so far. It's at an excellent price point compared to other smart watches. Also the seller seems reliable. I have no complaints with what I received. This is my first smart watch after a decade with various Fitbits. I wanted a basic health tracker with a few extra bells and whistles, but I did not want one to answer calls and text messages. PROS: The Fit3 synced easily with my Samsung S20. text: with customer service they would send us replacement. So, ok, we can deal with that. (Even though in the past few years you had to provide a ton of documentation including, IIRC, your drivers license.) I had a fitbit 6 that I got for Christmas 2024. Wow, a color screen. LOL. I needed a replacement as the screen on my Charge 5 kept getting dimmer, and dimmer, to the point when I could not read it outdoors and barely indoors. So I was gifted the Charge 6 and it was big step up. But it took me a few months until I realized that the floors option was gone, not just not working, but removed, to make space for other features I could care less about. So I looked around in late June and discovered the Fit 3. So I bought one. I am SO GLAD I did. What do I need? Step counter, heart rate, floors, distance, calories. Oh, and the kicker for me was AIRPLANE MODE. I study various energetic sciences including BioGeometry and the EMF coming from our devices is not benign. I don't wear a tracker to sleep, don't want anything live, and keep my blue tooth turned off unless I am actively syncing. This is what I see comparing Fit 3 to Fitbit Charge 6. What the Fit 3 has as an advantage: Larger screen, airplane mode, beautiful faces, much cleaner UI on the watch and in the android app(s) (I am using this with a Pixel 7 phone). The ability to sync only turning on bluetooth. A few years ago fitbit/google basically said we aren't syncing your fitbit unless you have both location/GPS and bluetooth turned on. Sometimes you could get the fitbit to sync with location off but this was tricky/spotty. What advantages did the Charge 6 have for me, personally. None really. It cost about 3x the amount for the Charge 6 compared to the Fit 3. That's ridiculous. In a head to head comparsion of measurements this is what I see. Fitbit Charge is often slower to start your step counts and the Fit 3 is quicker on that. So you steps counts are going to go up and perhaps the Fit 3 does count some arm motions as steps when it should not. But the Fit 3 is still very close (within 1-3%) with the Charge
product_name: SAMSUNG Galaxy FIT 3 [2024] 1.6" AMOLED Display | 14 Days Battery Life | 100+ Watchfaces | 100+ Exercise Modes | International Model - (Gray)

text: on that. So you steps counts are going to go up and perhaps the Fit 3 does count some arm motions as steps when it should not. But the Fit 3 is still very close (within 1-3%) with the Charge 6. The Fit 3 tends to undercount calories compared to the Charge 6, I can't tell which is more accurate. On the Charge 6 bluetooth syncing was always a laborious pain taking sometimes minutes to get the past few days of data and often having to turn bluetooth off/on and/or restarts to get it to sync. The Fit 3 is very fast *most* of the time. I did have one instance where the Fit 3 was not syncing in the Health App and I had to open the Wearable app and trigger a sync from there, which worked. The big thing is battery life. I have automatic activity tracking turned on and except when syncing in the evening I keep it on airplane mode. I charged it overnight last Thursday/Friday before I first put it on. It's now Wednesday evening, nearing 6 complete days of use and my battery level is at 55%. That frugal energy use is really good and is on par with the earlier monochrome fitbits. I am very pleased with my Fit 3 purchase. Back in the early to mid 2000's I was very excited about Timex was doing just in digital watches (Iron Man, etc). The Fit 3 harkens back to those kind of days for me when you could get a very full functionality wrist device that looked good, worked great, and was a good deal. This is definitely worth a close look if you don't need an higher priced fitness/communication device that is emitting EMFs into your pericardium meridian 24x7. Well done.

text: I use my fitbit inspire 3 everyday, mainly to track steps and heart rate. The extra features of Fitbit that require a premium subscription are just OK, and not really needed for me. I did try their free trial, but decided not to continue the premium service. The sleep monitoring is pretty neat though. For connectivity - It is really easy to setup with your phone with their app, but it just isn't for me. The step counter works great, and I use it to try and track my 10,000 steps a day, sometimes finding that I've come close to the goal just doing general work and movement during the day! I like the reminder vibration to remind you to get up and move every once in awhile (I'm sure you can disable this, but I don't). I went with this model of Fitbit because it is much smaller, and much lighter. The fit is really nice, and super lightweight. The screen is still big enough to read it, as the display is bright and clear. It doesn't work well if you want to do extras like messages/email etc, but I don't use it as a smart watch in that way. If you want those features, probably look for a larger model. The durability is another plus for this tracker. I wear it everyday, and without thinking about it (I forget I'm wearing it 99% of the time) I bump and scrape it on all types of things, especially at work because I work with my hands a lot. I can see scruffs and dings all over the edges now after a few months, but zero issues with the functionality, and no real damage to the screen or buttons. Seems that it will take a beating and be just fine. The other thing I really like about this tracker, and another reason I went smaller, is the battery life. I find I have to charge it maybe two or three times a month (at most). I don't really keep track, but it lasts a very long time, even though I wear it all day, everyday. I don't sleep with it anymore, so I'm sure that saves some battery. Either way, the battery lasts a long time, and charge time is very quickly through USB. My only concern is the waterproof type connector that it uses (a design that a lot of smart watches have) might wear down and require their proprietary cable. Because I haven't charged too many times and the charge lasts so long
product_name: Fitbit Inspire 3 Health &-Fitness-Tracker with Stress Management, Workout Intensity, Sleep Tracking, 24/7 Heart Rate and more, Midnight Zen/Black One Size (S & L Bands Included)

Overall, I really recommend the Inspire 3 is you want a lightweight, small, easy to read tracker. If you are mainly looking for steps/pulse, it is a great device. If you want some of the extras, maybe go a step up and expect to have a heavier device with shorter battery life. I purchased this to use mostly to track my steps and activity. The battery life is good and the step tracking seems to be accurate. It also has other features such as heart rate and other activity tracking, timer, stopwatch, alarm, find phone, and notifications. Everything seems to work without issue. The only reason I took a star off is because the auto wake option doesn t seem to work as consistently as i would like. Other than that, I feel like it is a good Fitbit watch especially for the price I paid. I now wear this instead of the Apple Watch that I purchased for about $200 more.

text: I'd been struggling with my Pixel 3 watch on longer runs it just couldn't hang in there past 17 miles, which was incredibly frustrating. I started looking for something with serious battery life but didn't want to feel like I was downgrading on cool features. The Forerunner 965 kept popping up, and I decided to give it a shot. Man, am I glad I did. I purchased the one with the white band, I think it looks very sleek and modern compared to the black one. Pros: Battery for Days (Well, Miles and Miles!): This is the biggest one for me. I can go on my long runs, and then some, without even thinking about the battery percentage. It's a huge relief not to have that anxiety anymore. I used to have to charge my pixel watch daily, I can go a full week without charging this watch! That Screen is Gorgeous: The AMOLED display is no joke. It's so bright and clear, even when I'm out in direct sunlight. Data is super easy to read at a glance, which is exactly what you need when you're trying to focus on your pace. Buttons I Actually Like Using: I didn't realize how much I'd appreciate good, tactile buttons until I had them. They're really responsive and make navigating menus or starting/stopping activities a breeze, especially with sweaty hands when a touchscreen can be finicky. Still Feels Smart: While it's definitely a running-focused watch, it still has a lot of the smart features I liked on my Pixel. I get my notifications, can control music, and it tracks all sorts of health metrics. Cons: Missing True Independence (No LTE): The only real drawback for me is the lack of LTE. I can't take calls or send texts if I don't have my phone with me. It's not a dealbreaker, as I'll wear my old Pixel watch with LTE on my other wrist to avoid me needing my bulky phone with me. Overall: Switching to the Forerunner 965 has been a game-changer for my running. The battery life alone makes it worth it, but the awesome screen and user-friendly buttons are fantastic bonuses. I finally get why so many runners swear by Garmin. If you're a runner who needs reliability and great performance without sacrificing too many smartwatch conveniences, I d definitely recommend checking this one out. Edit: Added a screenshot of my first run with the watch.
product_name: Garmin Forerunner 965 Running Smartwatch, Colorful AMOLED Display, Training Metrics and Recovery Insights, Black and Powder Gray, 010-02809-00

text: so many runners swear by Garmin. If you're a runner who needs reliability and great performance without sacrificing too many smartwatch conveniences, I d definitely recommend checking this one out. Edit: Added a screenshot of my first run with the watch. 10 miles and the battery only went down 3% throughout while listening to music. I made the right choice to switch! I love my Forerunner 965 because it fits into my life without trying to hype me up or guilt me. It s just there, quietly tracking things and giving me information I actually use. I like waking up and checking how I slept, not because I expect perfection, but because it explains why some days feel harder than others. When my sleep or HRV is off, it usually lines up with how I feel. That makes the data feel real, not gimmicky. Running with it feels natural. The screen is easy to read without thinking, even when I m tired or zoning out. I don t have to fight the watch to get what I need. Pace, heart rate, distance it s all just there when I glance down. I like that it doesn t force motivation. If I m ready to train, it confirms it. If I m not, it doesn t shame me it just says I might want to take it easy. Some days I listen, some days I don t. Either way, it adapts. I also like the small things: the battery lasting long enough that charging isn t part of my routine, the way trends build over time, and how progress shows up slowly instead of all at once. It makes improvement feel realistic. Phenomenal fitness watch. Forerunner 165 was my first Garmin after using Apple for many years. Found the 965 on sale after using the 165 for about a year and a half and went for it. So glad I did. Battery life is phenomenal, GPS tracking is great, watch is light yet feels solid. The titanium bezel really gives it a much more sturdy feel. Sits well on my arm. No issues sliding. Been about a month now and I m loving it.
product_name: Garmin Forerunner 965 Running Smartwatch, Colorful AMOLED Display, Training Metrics and Recovery Insights, Black and Powder Gray, 010-02809-00

text: I just received this smartwatch today and wanted to share my initial impressions while I m setting it up. Right out of the box, it looks really nice ~ sleek, lightweight, and more stylish than I expected for the price. I also like that it comes with two bands, which makes it easy to switch between a more casual and slightly dressier look. text: BATTERY LIFE I waited until I had to charge the keyboard before writing up this review. I received this the first week of December, charged it, and now this is 3/20/24 (almost 4 months later) and I am now charging it for the first time. I actually forgot that this was a rechargeable. I was looking for the battery slots and then remembered I had to plug it in. The keyboard light (red) lightning bolt had come on a few times but I figured I would just wait until the battery went dead. Then just a moment ago, I couldn't type any longer. As soon as I plugged it in where it's getting a charge, I was able to start using it immediately. I like that I can also turn it off when I go out of town so that it preserves the battery, although, admittedly I sometimes forget about this features. I use this keyboard daily as I do trading in the morning and I also do consulting work. I'm a techy person and just enjoy being on the computer. ERGONOMICS I personally love the feel of this type of keyboard with shallow keys that I can feel being pressed. I used to have a bluetooth HP keyboard that felt like this and went out, causing me to buy this one. It has a full keyboard and number pad. I use the Fn lock so that the volume, play controls work since I don't use the regular F keys as often. CONNECTION I had no issues connecting this to my computer. I plugged in the usb dongle, turned it on, and my laptop found it immediately. VALUE As of today, 3/20/24, this keyboard is only around $25 and I think this is a great value. CON The only con is that it isn't spillproof. My last keyboard, I accidentally dripped coffee onto it and it quit working. I believe this one will be the same, so I am trying to be very careful. Mine shipped directly from Arktek and arrived right away. It's been a great keyboard and I truly recommend it. GET THIS. I needed something quick because my keyboard failed in the middle of a workday and I didn't have time to look too deep. This had 3-hr delivery for me (delivered in 2). I DON'T REGRET MY CHOICE. text: Just received this keyboard and paired it with my computer. I love the style and lights but most of all the silence. My family accuses me of typing angry, hitting the keys hard. This keyboard is nearly silent! I love it! Here is to not being overstimulated by the sound of my typing at the end of the day! I was excited to see this keyboard show up and try it because Ive been wanting a nice wireless keyboard for a while now. I'm sort of left not knowing how to feel about it though. I really do love how clean and sleek it looks and how the keys feel when pressed. It's sort of solid and soft I don't know how to describe it. I usually like clicky ones but this just hits a spot where it's satisfying and feels good and really smooth to type. It's has various rgb modes for whatever you might want from just different solid colors or breathing patterns and flashing patterns and lighting different parts of the board with different colors. The brightness can be adjusted nicely with a nice range. There are also a lot of different options to connect the board to a computer also. There are three different Bluetooth channels to connect to multiple different devices and have them keep their connections and just switch channels to type on each device. I've successfully had it paired with my phone to type on and a computer also so it's nice. It has ha a channel to connect it with a cord normally the same one you'd use to charge it. There is also a sperate channel for connection using a USB dongle that come with the board also. There's so many different connection options but not all of them always work. I couldn't get the USB dongle to ever really connect to the computer no matter what I tried. I also couldn't get one of the Bluetooth channels to connect to anything either which is disappointing. Then the problem I have with the Bluetooth that does work is sometimes keys seem to stick down. Not the physical key but the trigger like walking forward in a game will co tinue walking forward sometimes even when I stop pressing the key. Also sometimes I need to press a key multiple times to get it to register which makes an impact on typing and in games also. The best experience I've had with using it is just directly connecting it with the USB cable but that sort of defeats the purpose of being a wireless keyboard. I love the feel of it so I continue to use it wired and only use wireless when what I'm doing

text: I've had with using it is just directly connecting it with the USB cable but that sort of defeats the purpose of being a wireless keyboard. I love the feel of it so I continue to use it wired and only use wireless when what I'm doing isn't a huge deal. For all those reason it landed at four stars for me but would really be a solid five if the Bluetooth and dongle connectivity issues were solved but as it is I'd say four stars. Product Overview This full size wireless keyboard supports tri mode connectivity (Bluetooth 5.0, 2.4GHz, and USB C wired), features quiet membrane switches, RGB backlighting, and an ergonomic curved layout. It s built with a spill resistant frame, smart indicator lights, and compatibility across Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android. Designed for both gaming and office use, it aims to deliver low latency performance with a modern aesthetic. Personal Experience The Bluetooth connection is extremely solid with very low latency, good enough that it feels close to a wired keyboard. The key feedback is impressive; it reminds me of a mechanical keyboard but quieter and smoother. The keys glide effortlessly, and the overall feel is a massive upgrade over my Razer BlackWidow V3. Everything is quiet except the spacebar, which sounds like a normal spacebar. Battery life is excellent when using Bluetooth or 2.4GHz. The ability to pair with multiple devices and switch between them instantly using keyboard shortcuts is a huge advantage. I can use it with my PC, TV, and smartphone without any delay. The RGB options are surprisingly good, with a mix of static and dynamic effects. Switching between them is instant and handled directly through keyboard shortcuts. One of the biggest highlights is the weight. This keyboard is heavy, which is a major plus; it doesn t slide around during intense gaming or fast typing. The fixed typing angle is also perfect. Since it doesn t use flip out legs, there s no risk of them collapsing mid game, and it naturally sits at the angle most people use anyway. Overall, the build quality, smooth key feel, multi device support, and weight make this feel like a high end keyboard. Final Verdict A feature rich, heavy, smooth typing wireless keyboard that punches way above its price. 5 stars.
product_name: MOFII Wireless Computer Keyboard with RGB Backlit, Tri-Mode Bluetooth 5.0/USB-C/2.4GHz Gaming Keyboard, Full Size Layout, Ergonomic Curved Design for Windows Mac PC Gamers & Office

text: Purchased to replace dead keyboard. I like the look and feel of this unit. The keys and lettering is large but not too large. The bluetooth connects easily and works very well. This is not a heavy keyboard, so if you use it on your lap like i do, its not uncomfortable. The keys are semi-quiet. They are not going to wake up the person next to you. They've got a good positive feel to them. This seems like it will last a while for an average user. In my opinion, its worth the price. I tried almost every bluetooth keyboards with the following features: - Have both Bluetooth and wireless dongle, I need to be able to access computer BIOS before bluetooth driver kicks in - Full size keyboard with number pad - Low profile, quiet keys - Layout support for both Mac and Windows (The Alt key and Command keys are the same) - Rechargeable There are a number of keyboards at this price range, and some have really nice design and packaging, but what most keyboards don't get right is having sensitive buttons. Specifically, if you tap too light or tap on the edge of the key, the button press would not register. You would think that they QA test these aspects before mass production but you'd be surprised. This is the only low priced keyboard with actually sensitive keys. I'm a light touch typer and I don't get any issues with this keyboard. So far the bluetooth connection is working solid and I'm not seeing any disconnections to my Macbook. Comparable keys are found form brands like Cherry or Logitech, but their keyboards are at least twice the price. If there's any negative feedback, it would be that the keyboard's plastic construction. It is relatively easy to bend, and the silver surface looks like silver colored plastic when competing brands either use an actual sheet of aluminum or a darker shade of gray that doesn't look as cheap. Looks aside, this keyboard is the most solid performer out of all the other bluetooth keyboards in this price range. This one is a keeper I ve been using the TECKNET Bluetooth Keyboard daily for the past few weeks, and I m thoroughly impressed. This is a standard U.S. full-size keyboard, and the layout is exactly what you d expect comfortable and familiar with all the right keys in the right places. The build quality is excellent. It doesn t feel cheap or flimsy like many keyboards in this price range. The keys are quiet, responsive, and

text: Quick ship! Love, love, love this keyboard. Love the colors and works well! This keyboard is amazing worked really well. I used it for about 4 1/2 years and then the number one key stopped working to my surprise. It had a five-year warranty. I contacted the manufacturer and they sent me a new keyboard. Everything works 100% with a new keyboard and then in fact a couple days later I received a second new keyboard I assume by mistake. Very great customer service. I did let them know. Definitely don t need a second good working keyboard. Will send it back if they ask! Recommend you all I bought this keyboard more than 2 years ago and it finally died. The battery stopped taking a charge. When I examined the unit, I saw that the seam along the top was split and it appears that the battery had blown up in size like as lipo's do when they die. I have used the unit about 40 or more hours a week so I feel I got my money's worth. I needed an immediate replacement and after shopping around on Amazon I found myself back looking to buy it again. It was priced at only about 60% of what I had payed for the first one. When I got the new one I was amazed at how much the quality had improved. The original one had moderately sloppy keys like a lot of cheaper keyboards. Sometimes when typing, especially a wider key, the key would be pressed at an angle and it would want to 'jam'. Not often, but still a bit annoying. The new one's keys are much more precise in their movement than the first keyboard's keys ever were. There is NO cocking sideways, or jammed movement. The keys are still light to the touch but they have a much more solid feel--no sideways play. This time I bought a 4 year warranty for it only to notice later that it already has a 5 year warranty (which I still haven't read). So I should attempt to get a replacement for the first unit which should still be under warranty. I plan to update this review with the outcome of that endeavor. So buy it already. In addition to the good price it has that long warranty and plenty of good features--numeric keys, wireless re-chargeable, sleep-mode, and the colorful back lighting. I have not used it for gaming or other 'high speed applications' but it should do well by you.

text: Looks just like this pictures and great quality! Unfortunately the mouse wasn t working, but I did message the company directly and they are sending me a new mouse! ORIGINAL REVIEW: I've been using this now for around 4 months and I deeply enjoy this set! The color is the perfect baby pink, soft enough to be almost salmon but also not. The ease of set up is wonderful, it's just plug and play. I did have to be careful about which USB slot I plugged it into, on my computer it can only be used properly in one of the USB plugs (my fault not the manufacturer). So if you have some functionality issues, try it in a different plug! I've not yet had to change the battery, so for four months on one set of batteries is pretty amazing. Especially since I don't turn it off (like I should) when I leave the office for the weekend. I use this at work and am constantly typing away or scrolling through documents using the wheel on the mouse. These have held up amazingly! I've not yet had to do a deep clean on them, but for only having been dusted for four months they still look fairly new. Again, despite constant regular 5 days per week use! The color still looks as fresh and new as it did the day it was delivered! All of the lettering is still beautifully visible with no smudges or marks of deterioration to the printed ink. I do like that it has a light to tell you when the battery is dying and needs to be changed, when I first installed the battery for the keyboard I made the mistake of using my old keyboards battery and it was just about dead - something I didn't know before I installed it! text: love this keyboard! it s cute, functional, quiet but sounds creamy. it s definitely a good price, very easy to use and easily connects to my macbook. I love the feel, sound, and functionality of this keyboard! It looks nice too. This is a great keyboard. It combines everything I wanted in a keyboard and then some. The cost was great for a bluetooth, wireless (corded is an option), multi-functional keyboard. First thing that sticks out to me is the overall/initial quality after using this about a week. It is the perfect amount of weight and does not feel cheap. Everything works like it should. Second thing is the sound of the keyboard. I type a lot daily for work and wanted a "membrane" that had a slightly softer or quieter keyboard typing experience. This gives you the perfect blend of "mechanical" keyboard style but without the loud, clacky sound that isn't fun for days where typing can be constant. Third, it works with all my devices pretty seamlessly. Disconnecting and reconnecting to this device is VERY quick. It was somewhat unexpected honesty, but a welcome surprise. The battery life is decent, I have had to charge it once in one week of use, so I expect on and off use for 8 hours per day, to need to charge this once per week with my current setup. Fourth, the placement of the keys initially I thought was a little odd, but you get used to it quickly when you come from a compact or full size keyboard, as this is some hybrid of both. It includes the num pad functionality of a larger keyboard and the compact style and size of smaller keyboards which is an awesome experience for me personally. Fifth, and last is personalization. It gives you a lot of control over built in functions with two "modes" Gaming mode and Work mode which change/allow you to control how the backlighting works and option to map custom functions to the buttons on the top right of the keyboard "M1" "M2" and "M3." The keyboard also allows you to add/change colors, different lighting movement patterns, cool reaction function with the lighting when typing, and the option to keep a solid color, the brightness of the backlight, or the option to completely shut it off completely if you want for something like saving battery. The two cons I could come up with for this product is the battery could be a bit better, like the higher 8,000
product_name: AULA S99 Gaming Keyboard, Wireless Computer Keyboards, Tri-Mode Bluetooth/2.4GHz/USB-C Custom Creamy Keyboard with Number Pad, RGB Backlit Media Knob Cute Programmable Keyboard for PC Gamer - Green

text: brightness of the backlight, or the option to completely shut it off completely if you want for something like saving battery. The two cons I could come up with for this product is the battery could be a bit better, like the higher 8,000 mAh battery Aula puts in their F99 wireless keyboard as this S99 is a 4,000 mAh battery. The second is that there is no "Home" button, likely to make the keyboard more compact. However, that is the annoying part. I use the Home and End buttons everyday for work, and the lack of having one all week was bothering me. I did find out however, that the Fn+1 (num pad #1 key) does the same thing as the End key and the Fn+7(num pad #7 key) does the same thing as the Home key, so I am back in business but it is still an annoyance to not have the dedidated key. TL;DR - Pros: 1. Good quality product 2. Great quieter keyboard sound. Louder than basic keyboards, quieter than the "clack" of mechanical keyboards 3. Device compatibility is seamless 4. Key placement somewhat odd, but keyboard overall has a good compact size 5. Good personalization options Cons: 1. Battery capacity may be an annoyance for higher usage (4,000 mAh) 2. No dedicated "Home" button. Found a workaround with turning num lock off and using Fn+1 and 7 keys on numpad.

text: I LOVE this keyboard!! After spending two weeks researching I decided on the Aula F99 and I am so happy with my decision. I m not a gamer but wanted a full keyboard for work and this checked all the boxes. The sound is so creamy and reminds me of marbles or smooth stones. It s so fun to type on and the RGB is kinda fun too. Bluetooth connected with ease, so did wired. Haven t tried the dongle yet but so far it s been plug and play play with no issues. Feels sturdy not cheap. Sounds great out of the box. Ordered the Gatreon Milky Yellow switches but I may not even need them cuz I m kinda digging this sound already. It s soooo satisfying. Great value!! I just received my shiny new mechanical keyboard today. I've been typing on it for some hours now and I must say that this keyboard is the BOMB! My fingers seem to glide across it while typing! The key caps are so smooth to the touch. I love that I can actually type faster without making as many typos as I would on a regular style keyboard. This keyboard is a game changer for anyone who needs to do heavy duty typing tasks. I must also add that it has the most addictive creamy clicking sound! And the RGB lights are really cool and fun to see. Another plus is the tri-mode connection feature. The USB receiver has a slot that stores it right on the keyboard itself. I really am pleased with the more compact size of this full keyboard to save room on top of the desk. It's substantial in its weight, not flimsy at all! I realize that I should have been buying a mechanical keyboard years ago because the other popular keyboards are not better than this one. Take note that you can change the keycaps anytime you want to change the look and style of your keyboard. You can only do this with a mechanical keyboard. The keycaps are not expensive either as I have already done research here on Amazon. I plan to purchase a couple of keycap sets in the future. Overall, I highly recommend this particular keyboard from AULA, I believe the price point is good for the quality of the keyboard. I would only recommend that they include the keycap tongs and a few gaskets with this keyboard as other manufacturers do. Bought this for my wife s WFH setup. She fell in love with the Creamy Switch craze on TikTok and this was a reasonably priced keyboard

text: This is a very high quality keyboard, I was pleasantly surprised. I have to run a legacy computer (a win xp if you can believe that) because it has some programs that can't be replaced and they will not run on anything newer than win xp. Well the idea of having a corded KB and mouse on my desk was a no brainier. I'm constantly switching back and forth between two computers and if one had a corded KB, it would be a total PITA. The ARTECK keyboard is slightly smaller than my original desktop KB and the key layout is slightly different, but it's not weird and is easy to get used to, the keys have a very nice touch to them and they're silent. IMHO I'd say the overall design is great... both functionally and aesthetically. Connecting the RF link between the KB and the computer is a piece of cake, no surprises... it just works. The power for the KB is somewhat of a mystery and it's not explained well in the instructions (however there are instruction included) I'm guessing there's a battery on the main circuit board that can be charged using the supplied USB cable. There's a small on/off switch on the face of the KB and few LED's indicating when the KB is first powered on, or if it's locked, or when the battery needs to be charged... This is easily a 5 star product, easy to recommend, but a KB is a personal thing and you may not agree... for the cost, I don't think you can go wrong with this product. Cheers. The keyboard is very sleek. No stickiness at all. Keyboard size is very functional and typing speed is enhanced. It is very durable and the quality is great. I like the rechargeable feature as it is a wireless keyboard. Love it. This is my second keyboard from Arteck, and I m very satisfied with it. It s affordable, easy to use, and has a clean, simple design. It looks great and is lightweight, making it perfect for traveling between home and the office. I would definitely recommend it.
