text: Excellent customer service. They took care of my problem. The product is very satisfactory. It types well and has a great look. I highly recommend it! I just got this keyboard yesterday, and I'm in love with it already. I love tactile keyboards, but most mechanical ones are too loud for my liking. Plus my mic keeps picking up the sound of my old keyboard and it was annoying. I was looking at Logitech's mechanical keyboard, but I wasn't sure about paying $140 for a bland-looking keyboard. I watched demos for this keyboard and was sold on the sound quality and customization. This keyboard is exactly as described for an excellent price. Plenty of color options and designs to choose from. Great battery life if used wirelessly and turning it off when not in use. The keys are soft, not as loud as the typical mechanical keyboards, but rather a soft clicky sound. It's like ASMR to my ears, honestly. The keyboard is also comfortable to type with and easy to customize. I just got this keyboard and I absolutely plan to make an update here in 3-6 months, but right out the gate this board is GORGEOUS. The board itself is a bit chunky(in comparison to a membrane keyboard), fairly average for any mechanical keyboard. In terms of space, its a wonderful downgrade from a full sized keyboard without sacrificing any of the convenient keys. It contains an fkey row and a screen and knob without extending the top of the board with a lot of negative space. The feel and sound of the keys are probably one of my favorite parts of the board. They're bright and chirpy and muted. They aren't loud with a very relaxing but outgoing resonance. The ombre on the keys is very aesthetic and the key marks are on the side of the key maximizing the aesthetic without losing functionality. They keys themselves as well as the switches are swappable! I'm at rest knowing if I stop enjoying the performance of this board, I can vamp it up by changing the feel and appeal. The screen on the keyboard eliminates the need to install a third party driver as all the rgb is programmable within the board itself. It does offer a driver for even more customizability. Between Size of the board, functionality, appearance, sound, feel and connection options this is absolutely worth the price. especially compared to other name brand boards that typically see sacrifices in size or swappable switch abilities. 5/5 I will be

text: I don't often leave reviews, but this board is something else. After the web software settings have been configured, this keyboard is incredible. Originally, I ordered the Galleon 100 SD, which was bricked on arrival. I ordered this one, and it was delivered the next day. I've found it to be a more feature-packed keyboard than the Galleon 100 SD if it did not have a physical stream deck integrated. First, the Vanguard Air 99 is fully wireless with up to three Bluetooth devices at one time, in addition to wired and slipstream options. It has the Stream Deck S-keys, which work with infinite customization. The keys themselves are like no other; they are shorter and lighter than traditional mechanical keyboards but are larger and feel better than laptop keys, with almost no pressure required for input. Important considerations to note include the common complaint that the Corsair Web Hub software is finicky, and having Corsair Web Hub and Stream Deck or iCUE open at the same time can cause PC freezes. The solution to this is only having one open at a time or closing one app every few minutes. But once it's configured, the keyboard is like no other board I've ever seen. Absolutely buy if you want customization, the mechanical feel with significantly less pressure required for input, dual software connectivity with infinite customization via Stream Deck integration, a quiet typing experience, and lightweight portability. Oh, and it has the 8k polling rate just like all of the other high-end keyboards on the market, but with significantly more variability in other polling options (up to 4k wireless, 8k plugged in). Item was released without full support or functioning firmware. Battery drains fast, keybinds and assignments will not save, random keys will latch and continue to be selected. Very nice. Sleek light weight compared to a lot of other keyboards out there. Only down side is no icue support yet. LCD screen is very bright a clear Same with the leds on the key caps.
product_name: Corsair Vanguard AIR 99 Wireless Low-Profile Optical-Mechanical RGB Gaming Keyboard QWERTY US, 99% Layout, OPX Switches, 8000Hz Hyper-Polling, Stream Deck Integration, Flashtap SOCD Black

text: This Logitech Signature MK650 Combo is a solid choice for anyone needing a reliable, quiet, and comfortable setup. I use it in a shared workspace, and the quiet typing and clicking have made a noticeable difference no more noisy distractions during calls or meetings! The SmartWheel mouse is another highlight it automatically switches between smooth scrolling and precise control, which is great when switching between spreadsheets and web browsing. The ergonomics are also impressive: the keyboard has a soft palm rest, and the mouse fits naturally in the hand. It's clear Logitech put a lot of thought into comfort and function. One major plus for me is how easy the low-pressure keys are to press. If you're dealing with carpal tunnel, arthritis, or general hand mobility issues, this keyboard makes a real difference. Typing feels smooth and effortless, which reduces strain during long work sessions. I use it with Windows without issues, and the Logi Bolt connection has been stable and secure throughout. A few drawbacks: there s no backlighting, so it's not ideal for late-night work. It s also battery-powered (AA), which isn't a dealbreaker thanks to the long battery life, but I d prefer rechargeable. Finally, while Logi Bolt is great for stability, it does mean you need a USB port for the dongle unless you're using Bluetooth something to consider for laptop users with limited ports. Overall: A great value combo for professionals who want a quiet, ergonomic, and accessible setup. Nearly perfect just a few minor trade-offs. I'm using this with a Dell XPS 13. My previous keyboard/mouse combo only had the nano USB receiver (which is also an option with this combo), but I had occasional lag issues. I decided to get the MK650 and try it with Bluetooth. I think it's a little expensive for what you get, but it's been worth it. It connects instantly and the connection is stable, plus it frees up a USB port on my hub. I have had zero issues with lag, drops, or interference, even with other Bluetooth devices connected such as earbuds or speakers. Keyboard: I'm used to chiclet style keys, but this is fine. text: 4-21-22 First my apologies to Artek for the delay in updating my review. I promised myself and them I would do so. After I wrote my initial review below, Arteck immediately contacted me through Amazon and offered to send me a replacement keyboard free. They sent me a code for one on Amazon and it arrived a day later. It was fully charged, which surprised me as not all of these do arrive this way. This keyboard worked amazing. No issues what so ever. It connected to all 3 of devices (my windows 10 PC, laptop and android phone) without a hitch. I've been using it non-stop since April 1st and been able to switch between all 3 devices seemlessly. There is a bit of a delay in the connection, but honestly, the 2 second delay occasionally that occurs is nothing. I've learned to adjust my habits a bit and wait and I'm good to go. I'm pretty sure most Bluetooth muliconnect keyboards suffer from this glitch... but either way I'm happy with this one. The customer service of this company alone has been outstanding and they have done everything they could to make sure I was happy. I would not only purchase this keyboard again, especially with the warranty it has, but I would purchase any product that Arteck sells and give it a chance. Initial impressions out of the box is that it's a nice looking and feeling keyboard. It doesn't feel cheap. They give you a micro USB cable to charge the keyboard with. It doesn't illuminate other than the LEDs above the buttons for multidevice. It's fairly easy to connect your devices to. Which is good, because you'll be reconnecting them often in an attempt to troubleshoot this device. I owned this product for less than a week. 3 days is all I had of a honeymoon period before it started giving me trouble. After that, it would randomly start going to sleep on me and refused to reconnect to my devices properly when it woke up. I checked all of my device settings (I'm in IT support for a living so yes I know what I'm doing). In an effort to be thorough, I even reinstalled my Bluetooth drivers on my 2 devices etc. It was a fruitless effort. This keyboard just didn't want to work properly. text: Heavier than I thought it would be and it feels premium for the low price tag. There are tons of color options for the RBG and you can even write your own to fully customize. I tried multiple keyboards and finally landed on this one. I tried a Royal Kudge mechanical it was too small, I tried a generic mechanical keyboard from Amazon - the keys were soft and not clicky, this keyboard is like the old school keyboards from the 80s, 90s. It reminds me of the sound the old Apple computer keybards and commodore 65 keyboards - which is why I got it. I have another mechanical keyboard but it is wired - tht one is more like a classic keyboard but it clicks lound and the keys have a satisfying depth. This keyboard is unique b/c it looks like an old school typewriter and you can see between and under all the keys - I wonder what it will look like when it is dirty. it has taken a bit to get used to the size - some of the keys feel much further than when I use my laptop or other keyboards. It has a beautiful range of lighting schemes, and it was out of the box ready to use. The other thing I love about it - it's is substatial, it feels heavy, very solid as it sits on the desktop - this will not move around or slip - some of my other keyboards slipp all over my desk. if you like clicky keyboards get this one. This keyboard fixed all of those issues. There are no sidewalls for dust and hair to get stuck in, which can be a pro and a con. text: This is my second time purchasing this keyboard. It works well and is well worth the price! Highly recommend! This is the third one of these I've had over a stretch of many years. Eventually the letters wear out and they get dirty, though the keyboards still work. I finally got this because at the price (less than $20!) it's the computer equivalent of having that "new car smell". Since I didn't get the combo with the wireless mouse, I have to use two USB receivers, but since I have a USB extension for outboard drives that had available slots, it wasn't a problem to use this with my old mouse. update: Even though the keyboard is less than 12 feet from the computer, I have poor connectivity and must raise the keyboard above the comforter and point it within like a 65 degree arc of the computer for it to actually receive the keystrokes. Aside from that, I'm good, as described below: I'm a pretty fast typist. (~80 wpm) and I translate japanese light novels while lying in bed. I need a keyboard that's efficient, sturdy, has an intuitive key layout, and is physically stable while resting on a blanketed lap. Additionally, I want a keyboard that use a user-replaceable battery, because failed proprietary batteries make the device itself fail, and product failure due to passage of time is unacceptable. Compared to my last wireless keyboard, this one is lighter while being comparably rigid, and the key action registration effort is FAR more consistent and efficient than my last. Additionally, keystrokes are registered on the screen without any lag or missed keys. With such a great price and no issues with performance, this easily scores 5 stars. My biggest criticism is that I wish the backspace key were larger. It's not as horribly small as that of a lot of laptops, but not as easy to strike as the ergonomic keyboard which sits at the desktop computer.
product_name: Microsoft Wireless Keyboard 850 Special Edition with AES - Black. Wireless Keyboard. Snap-In USB Transceiver

text: I'm loving this keyboard! I'm having trouble deciding whether to call it "full-size" or not. On my desktop keyboard, the keys measure 11" across. On the TeckNet wireless keyboard, the keys measure 10.75" across. So for my purposes, it is a full-size keyboard. As such, I have had no adjustment period with this keyboard. Of course, they key travel isn't the same as a desktop keyboard. But if you have every used an ultrabook keyboard, then this will feel instantly familiar. The keyboard is light, but the rubber feet on the back keep it stable on your desk/table while typing. The keyboard uses a USB receiver which connected and worked flawlessly. The USB receiver stores in the battery compartment on the back of the unit. Two AAA batteries power the keyboard and there is an ON/OFF switch on the back. The function key row also doubles as the media keys, Home, Search, etc. Funny side note - I just tried the alternate function for the F10 key. There is a power symbol (that would be PC power, not Black Power) on the key and I expected a window to pop up giving me power down choices. But instead it just instantly put my PC to sleep. I was kind of freaking out because I thought it had shut down and I had lost all my work. But it was just sleeping. I haven't used the keyboard long enough to honestly evaluate the battery life. The company says up to 6 months on the battery and they say it has a smart sleep mode to conserve battery for those times that you forget to turn the switch to off. There is also a low power indication on the keyboard - the caps lock light will change to red and flash when it is time to change the batteries. All-in-all this is a great little keyboard at a great price. Why did you pick this product vs others?: I work from home but also travel frequently for work, and being away from my usual setup always impacts my productivity. So, I ve been upgrading my travel gear after getting a portable external monitor, I realized I also needed a solid keyboard and mouse. I m a bit old school and prefer battery-powered devices over rechargeable ones (I always forget to charge them!), so I was specifically looking for a set that uses batteries. This keyboard and mouse are the perfect size for travel, super easy to pack, and I love that they both connect using a single USB

text: I was using Nexigo 4k and it struggled to blend the green screen around my edges and so I bought 2 of these to replace my 2 4k cameras. The 4k cams served me well, but this 1080p cam just gets a clearer quality. When I was replacing the 4k cam, I hooked both cams up and used them as reference checks for when I was adjusting my out of the box ObsBot camera software settings. If you need something with a stable image and is easy to install and it just functions and autofocuses for you really well and can blend with the green screen to give you really good quality, then you should definitely pick these up because the price is super great for this level of quality. What I'm trying to say is that this camera looks better than my 4k camera did and it's not even close in comparison. I got a slightly sharper image with the 4k camera but it just struggled with the green screen and sudden movement when I dance around during my DJ live sets, and this one does not. And it works great for gaming, too. But, I cannot really attached for the sound quality because I don't use it for that I have my own mic for all that, but from what I did see is that it was clear but like I said I just use my own mic. You should definitely get this camera because it's super cheap and it's super powerful! Upgrade your stream! This camera is wonderful for what it costs. If you're looking to get a webcam, if you need a camera for streaming/content creation, get this one, it'll get you what you need without costing you an arm and a leg. A lot of cameras can have diminishing returns the more you spend, and this feels like it's right at the point of spending the amount of money for the quality you're getting, rather than overspending for less and less benefit over time. The mount for the camera is great, and it holds it onto the monitor well without easily slipping, although make sure you're careful when you grab the camera lens cover, because if you're too rough with it, it'll definitely fall off. (although additional features past that are going to require their dedicated software) However, make sure your lighting allows it to work properly. It works on every computer/laptop I've tried it on, running both Windows and Linux, so that's nice as well. It's definitely a great camera for the price, and I think 99% of people won't need anything better than this in terms of camera quality. I don't think I'm going to be the best person to show you the actual visual quality considering the lighting and space I have available to me though, so I'd suggest looking up some videos on YouTube with shots from this camera in different lighting setups, since that should give you a much better idea of its quality. text: Purchased 4 months ago and love this product. Truly plug and play. Not only does it easily connect and charge my apple watch, earbuds and phone (iPhone 16 with gel case) but it is the best thing ever for traveling. Whereas I used to have to make sure to either pack the appropriate cords for each devise or share a charge cable for multiple devices, now I just fold up this charge mat and toss it in my luggage. Folded up it is about 3"x3"x1" plus the single cable required. Unfolded on my nightstand it's about a foot long. The devices magnetically find their location automatically and I have no trouble attaching nor detaching devices, even in the dark. The watch charger can pivot up to charge the watch on its side but I just keep it flat and lay my watch on it. I also appreciate that it comes in different colors. I don't like white peripherals that quickly look dirty and I don't prefer black peripherals that can show dust. I went with the dark blue color and after four months of daily use plus multiple business trips, it still looks brand new and works perfectly. I would recommend this to anyone who has the three Apple devices and especially to anyone who travels with those three Apple devices. Honestly, this should have been something that Apple offered but glad that an aftermarket manufacturer was able to provide and at a very reasonable price. text: Jackery Explorer 2000 V2 Review: I'm referring to the equipment as portable power station / PPS / battery. PERFORMANCE: It arrived well packaged and in perfect condition, with 30% charge. Charging was very simple. There is a quiet charging mode, which takes 3x duration to full charge, but is also quieter. I thought full charge speed was a nice, quiet, pleasant white / no need for the quiet charge option. Quiet charging is unneeded for us, but a neat feature. I didn't realize when shopping that there was a light on the power station (dim, bright, SOS pattern options), so that's good. I created a Jackery account and paired the device with the app, which was all simple. I guess the best part of the app is being able to remotely see the % battery left. For what it's worth though, you can remotely turn on each segment of the power station (DC / USBs, AC / normal wall outlets, and the light) too. We unplugged the fridge from the wall and plugged it into the PPS. Treated the fridge as we normally do, opening when we needed to take something out / put something in, dispensing water. It lasted for 21.3 hours. The battery also has pass-through charging, meaning you can plug the PPS into the wall while power is on, and the fridge (or other equipment) will charge from the battery. If we're away from the house while the power comes back on, the fridge will switch to being powered from the wall (via the PPS) while the battery starts to recharge in preparation for the next power loss event. The charging speed during pass-through charging is roughly the same as charging while not powering anything (~1500W). We also tested pass-through charging by turning the breaker off (simulating power loss) while the fridge was plugged into the PPS and the PPS plugged into the wall. The transition from pass-through to battery was seamless. The fridge lights never even flickered through wall-power loss and through changing back to pass-through charging. Given this capability, I could see using it as a battery backup for our sump pump, with the sump pump always plugged into the PPS, which would always be plugged into the wall. _________(I haven't dont research to verify if there will be any effect on life of product if used that way.)__________ The alternative is the P

At the time, Jackery was selling for $900 (no coupon). I found the Jackery Explorer 2000 V2 to be comparable, but ~$100-200 lower cost than competitors mentioned above. Dec6, 2024: Amazon is back to selling for the regular listed price of $1500, with no sale. Jackery is back to the regular listed price of $1500, but with a $600 "coupon" automatically applied at checkout. This sale is the "Christmas Sale!" December 3-8. So Jackery's Black Friday sale wasn't exclusive to Black Friday. Dec12, 2024: Amazon: sale for $900 Jackery: sale for $1100 (listed at $1500, with $400 coupon) LONGEVITY: I have only had this product for about a week, so I can not speak to the longevity of the product. I tested this during a FL power outage and it worked very well to charge phones, power a fan and a laptop. It's powerful enough to power a refrigerator for a whole day. The unit is heavy but easy to move with the handle. It's has a fan for cooling the batteries while changing, but it's not loud. text: A great and sturdy design. Solid build and surprisingly light weight and portability. A bit steep cost of entry but worth it if you plan on using daily as an EDC for your laptop. This coming from an audio pro...I use along with a full-sized keyboard and mouse and it makes the setup work ergonomically like a charm! Worthwhile purchase. Very packable and the included pouch does the trick perfectly. I used the original roost stand from way back when it launched on Kickstarter many years ago. I have also used the v2 stand for many years as my primary stand for work at my desk and while traveling. I was curious about this latest iteration and bought it to compare. I also did a comparison against the Nexstand. In my opinion, this latest Roost stand is the best version yet. The Nexstand doesn't compare to any of the Roost stands, being larger to pack, flimsier, and less adjustable. This latest version of the Roost has the most height settings of any of the iterations and folds up slightly more compactly to boot. The only disadvantage compared to the v2 is that you can't adjust the height on the fly, you need to remove the laptop and adjust the height before adding the laptop again. That said, the slight additional compactness, sturdiness and height adjustments (3 vs 7) make this iteration the best. If you can afford the cost, this is the best portable laptop stand to use with a keyboard and trackpad (or mouse) by quite a bit. Worth every penny in my book.
